---
title: "ATI Big-Desktop"
date: 2006-11-17
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-11-17
[FONT=Verdana][SIZE=4]**Dual Monitor Support With Binary, ATI-Only Big-Desktop**[/SIZE][/FONT]     [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]The following HowTo attempts to enable Dual Monitor support by enabling the Big-Desktop function of the Ati-only binary graphics driver.

[/FONT]  [/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]**[SIZE=3]System Requirements:[/SIZE]**[/FONT][/LEFT]
[INDENT][LEFT][FONT=Verdana]ONE Dual-Output ATI graphics card
Functional Fglrx Driver.[/FONT][INDENT][FONT=Verdana]The following guide explains how to install and enable the fglrx driver:    [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI)[/FONT][/INDENT][/LEFT]
[/INDENT][LEFT][FONT=Verdana][SIZE=3][B]Let's get started!!!

[/B][U][SIZE=2][COLOR=Red] Update: Instead of Manually editing the Xorg file, I've decided to attempt to write a HowTo based on the "aticonfig" tool.  So, for about a week or so, I need individuals to try out this method and report the outcome.  You can continue to post in this thread, or you can send me a private message to report the result. So, here goes nothing!

[/COLOR][/SIZE][/U][/SIZE][I][COLOR=Red][B] 1. I know you have already saved a back of the xorg.conf file (you did, didn't you? :D).:

[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]

2. Next, we want X to be using the fglrx driver, so:

[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
sudo aticonfig --initial --overlay-type=Xv
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]

3. Now that the xorg.conf is fit to be edited, enter the following commands:

[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
sudo aticonfig --desktop-setup=horizontal --sync-vsync=on --add-pairmode=Width0xHeight0+Width1xHeight1
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]

Here's what each command means:
[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
--desktop-setup=horizontal
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B] this command enables Big-Desktop, and places the two monitors next to each other (left-right orientation).  Other arguments that could replace it are:
[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
--desktop-setup=horizontal,reverse <= flips your screens.
--desktop-setup=vertical <=places your monitors in a up-down orientation.
--desktop-setup=vertical,reverse <=flips your screens.
--desktop-setup=clone
--desktop-setup=single

``````
--sync-vsync=on 
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]this command prevents tearing during 3D rendering.
[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
--add-pairmode=Width0xHeight0+Width1xHeight1
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B] this command tells X what are the resolutions for Monitor1+Monitor2.  

4. Now, you can experiment with your monitors without restarting X. First, you need to see how X identifies your monitors:

[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
sudo aticonfig --query-monitor
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]

Then based on the information provided by the query monitor command, replace STRING in the following command:

[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
sudo aticonfig --enable-monitor=STRING,STRING
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]The values of STRING should be one of these:
            [/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
[INDENT] none
crt1
crt2
lvds
tv
tmds1
tmds2[/INDENT]
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B] 5. If the enable-monitor command works for you, then you need to add it to the config file:

[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT]```
sudo aticonfig --force-monitor=STRING,STRING
```[FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B]

Again replace STRING with the values given by query-monitor.

5. Now restart X (ctrl+alt+backspace) or reboot your computer.
[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT][INDENT][FONT=Verdana]*[COLOR=Red]**Note: the --enable-monitor command DOES NOT change your configuration file (xorg.conf).  Therefore when you restart X or your computer, it may or may not work.**[/COLOR]*[/FONT]
[/INDENT][FONT=Verdana][I][COLOR=Red][B][SIZE=3][COLOR=Blue]The following is how to manually configure Xorg.conf file. It will soon become obsolete (I just need more input from those who have test the previous method).

[/COLOR][/SIZE]1. Open up Xorg.conf using ONE of the following commands:[/B][/COLOR][/I][/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Gnome (Ubuntu):[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
gksudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]KDE (Kubuntu):[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
kdesu kate /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Xfce4 (Xubuntu):[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
gksudo mousepad /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Command-Line Based:[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]***[COLOR=Red]2. This is about the simplest HowTo, simply copy the following lines to your "Device" Section:[/COLOR]***[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana][COLOR=Red][B][I]3. Save the file, and kill X (ctrl+alt+backspace), or reboot.

[/I][/B][/COLOR][/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Now dual monitor support should be enabled. If not, then please post your problems.[/FONT][/LEFT]

---

### Post by vegardh on 2006-11-23
Edit: erm, resolved, you just need to change the resolution to 2560x1024 under System->Preferences.

I have one problem with this and Gnome: After I log in, I can just see the pointer in the edge of the secondary screen, not move into it, so apparently the setup works, but something constraints the movement. 

Has Gnome or Metacity some setting that affects this? On the login-screen, I can move the pointer on both screens, i.e. all OK there. 

I'm on Dapper. The card is a 9600 Mobility.

My Device section, even though I doubt it has anything to do with it:

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
EndSection

### Post by marcele on 2006-12-29
Here is my working xorg.conf with bigdesktop

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier     "Default Layout"
        Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
        InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "Files"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
        FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load  "i2c"
        Load  "bitmap"
        Load  "ddc"
        Load  "dri"
        Load  "extmod"
        Load  "freetype"
        Load  "glx"
        Load  "int10"
        Load  "type1"
        Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"
        Option      "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver      "synaptics"
        Option      "SendCoreEvents" "true"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/psaux"
        Option      "Protocol" "auto-dev"
        Option      "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
        Identifier  "stylus"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
        Option      "Type" "stylus"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
        Identifier  "eraser"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
        Option      "Type" "eraser"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
        Identifier  "cursor"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
        Option      "Type" "cursor"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "HSync2" "30-82" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
        Option      "VRefresh2" "56-76" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
        Option      "Mode2" "1440x900"
        Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,LVDS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Virtual   1440 900 
                Viewport  0 0
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

You can change the "mode2" and "virtual" numbers to whatever your second monitor's resolution is.

Also you can change from dual screen to single screen (and vise versa) by using xrandr.

$ xrandr    #this will display all available modes
$ xrandr -s 0  #replace zero with any mode that you want to change to


Some notes:
1. Currently it impossible to get beryl or compiz working with bigdesktop.
2. If your resolution for your second display is larger than your primary then your mouse pointer will be misaligned on your primary monitor (its a current bug with the ati drivers).

---

```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us,bg"
	Option	    "XkbVariant" ","
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "grp:alt_shift_toggle,lv3:ralt_switch,grp_led:scroll"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier  "Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
	HorizSync   30.0 - 65.0
	VertRefresh 50.0 - 75.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID	    "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option "Mode2"         "1680x1050" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen[0]"
	Device     "Device[0]"
	Monitor    "Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Virtual 1680 1050
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

Make sure after you reboot from following this tutorial you go to System > Preferences > Screen Resolution and change your resolution to 2560x1024 (or similar).

### Post by Ziox on 2007-01-05
Sorry about my prolong absence. 
Now to work:

I have never really understood or use the "Virtual" option.  From my understanding, it simply allows your screen size to expand beyond your monitor's capable resolution.  However, since both of your (kEinstein) monitors have the correct resolution.  I would not use the virtual option at all.  Simply value the "Virtual" to 0 or remove the option all together.  Instead, add a "Modes" line similar to the following example:

```
"Modes" "1680x1050" "1440x1050"
```Add that line to your 'Screen' section.

Also, note this.  The "Mode2" option does not necessarily apply to what you perceive the be "second screen"  Each graphics card drivers detect and label heads (of graphics cards) differently, therefore, what you perceive as the "second" screen might be presented in the conf file as the "first/primary" screen. This might come into play when your remove the Virtual Option.

As with this comment:
"1. Currently it impossible to get beryl or compiz working with bigdesktop."

It isn't completely correct.  From what I've heard, XGL will work on dual monitors if the total screen resolution is under 2048x768.  Personally, I was able to enable XGL with the open-source driver "ati" and dual-screen enabled by "MergedFB".  

For a screenshot of it, check here: _[http://ubuntuforums.org/gallery/showphoto.php/photo/3701/ppuser/139177](http://ubuntuforums.org/gallery/showphoto.php/photo/3701/ppuser/139177)_

---

### Post by guyg on 2007-01-08
I spent some time getting my setup to work, so I thought I'd share the configuration with others. My setup is:
1. 1600x1200 laptop monitor
2. 1680x1050 external monitor
3. Adapter: ATI FireGL Mobility T2  
4. fglrx driver and BigDesktop for dual-head support

First, a couple of notes regarding what did and didn't work:
1. PairMode was necessary to support the different resolutions
2. The 'Virtual' line in the 'Screen' section was also necessary. Note how it is constructed: The screen resolutions are 1600x1200 and 1680x1050, and the Virtual line has 1680x1200 -- the maximum length in each axis.

In xorg.conf, I added the following:

[INDENT]
[FONT="Courier New"]Section "Device"
[INDENT]        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"

        # --- The following was added for dual-head support:
        Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" # Enable Big Desktop
        Option "PairMode"         "1600x1200+1680x1050" # Pair mode resolutions
        Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO"
        Option "HSync2" "65.29" #The horizontal sync for the secondary display.
        Option "VRefresh2" "59.954" #The refresh rate of the secondary display.
[/INDENT]EndSection[/FONT]
[/INDENT]

And also:

[INDENT][FONT="Courier New"]Section "Screen"
[INDENT]        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
                Virtual 1680 1200 # Note the resolutions in this line
        EndSubSection[/FONT]
[/INDENT]EndSection
[/INDENT]

Next goal is to get 3D to work :)

@ guyg: It is not that I don't believe you, but ;)  actually you used the same method I do - and it does not work for me (according to xorg.conf sections). Would you mind posting your xrandr settings?
Mine actually look like this: 
```
keinstein@thinkpad:~$ xrandr
 SZ:    Pixels          Physical       Refresh
*0   3360 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )  *60  
 1   1680 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 2   1400 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 3   1280 x 1024   ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   70   60  
 4   1280 x 768    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 5   1152 x 864    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   70   60  
 6   1024 x 768    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   72   70   60  
 7   1024 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 8    848 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 9    800 x 600    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   72   70   60   56   47

 12   640 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   72   60

```

I get the 3360x1050 option - not 3080x1050...

In your case, output from xrandr should quote (1680+1600= ) 3280x' your vertical px ' - presumably!
But, if it says sthg like 3360x'whatever' I fear you will be missing 80px of your horizontal screen.

Another way to check this: 
1) Maximize two windows on both of your screens
2) Make a screenshot of your entire screen and look for the very right 80px :-| 

For me, there are 280px not filled with nautilus i.e. you can see the background image, although both windows are expanded to your screens' pysical capabilites...

I'd be to happy if it worked for you!!! Don't get me wrong - i've been playing around for some time now. I doubt every working solution ](*,) until I find out for myself what's going wrong here...

The second (external) screen is on the right side. A window is supposedly maximized - at least on-screen :confused:

And here is the xorg.conf
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "Mode2"        "1280x1024"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,CRT"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "31-60"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "56-75"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

All I can say is, help, i've never seen anything like this.
Also of note I've had other problems getting dual monitor support working with:
mergeFB [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1984281&postcount=15]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1984281&postcount=15")
Xinerama [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1984799&postcount=41]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1984799&postcount=41")

My gfx card is an ati radeon xpress 200m, the external monitor is a goodmans LD1701 and I'm using Edgy 64bit.
Yet it works fine in Windows :-(

### Post by kEinstein on 2007-01-09
Hi! This is just a quick suggestion as I am in my windoze partition surfing the forums. 

subminimal: sthg very similar happened to me when messing around heavily with xorg.conf. I don't remember exactly what caused the problem - nor can I reproduce it at the time of writing - but if you have time, consider the following:
(For the relevant sections, xorg.conf looks quite similar to mine.)

1) Try to avoid the sync range and be more specific.
```
	Option	    "HSync2" "31-60"  # e.g. 49
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "56-75"  # e.g. 60
```

2) Have you already used 'PairMode' in the Devive Section?
```
	Option	    "PairMode"        "1280x1024+1280x1024"  # instead of "Mode2"   "1280x1024"
```

(and don't forget to check the screen resolutions after rebooting: $ xrandr)

Probably this will get you somewhere...

---

### Post by ignignokt00 on 2007-01-09
I'm trying to use my TV from my s-video out on my video card as my 2nd monitor.  
Any special tips?  I can't seem to get any results.

edit:  I actually got it working just fine using the tips on this page.  Thanks.
The pair mode did the trick.

       Option      "VRefresh2" "60"

### Post by kEinstein on 2007-01-19
Hi there! I'm sorry for the long absence in writing. Unfortunately I had to prepare for my last exam - but now that it's over, I had some time to spend on xorg.conf, dualhead with different resolutions, successfully!

@subliminal: hope you find the appropriate information in here! Actually, I ran over your issue (blank terminals - no reponse) but this might do the trick! Get back with your results! 
[SIZE="4"]
HowTo set up ATi BigDesktop using fglrx with DIFFERENT screen resolutions in pairmode[/SIZE]

Ziox has made great effort in demonstrating different [dualhead/bigdesktop/mergedFB/twinview capabilities]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=221174") of ATi and nVidia cards. Nevertheless, some of us have been struggling to set up BigDesktop with different screen resultions. In my case there are two monitors:
1) an internal LCD screen @ 1400x1050
2) an external monitor @ 1680x1050
The crucial point is setting 'pairmode' correctly in your xorg.conf, forget any 'virtual' or 'mode2' settings...

[SIZE="3"]NOTE 1: pairmode is supported in versions >= 8.28.8 of fglrx!!![/SIZE]

[SIZE="3"]NOTE 2: apply changes in screen resolution where necessary[/SIZE]

Start with having a look at aticonfig's current pairmode capabilites:
```
$ aticonfig --list-pairmode
Get pair modes : 1
 0    1680x1050 + 1680x1050 --> 3360x1050 
```

Although configured in xorg.conf with
```
Section "Device"
       "PairMode" "1400x1050+1680x1050" 
EndSection
```
1400x1050 + 1680x1050 --> 3080x1050 does not show up. 

So, let's backup current xorg.conf as we are going to mess around with it:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.failsafe
```

Then, tell aticonfig to enable additional pairmode capabilites as it does not recognize them via xorg.conf (aticonfig makes some changes to xorg.conf now):
```
$ sudo aticonfig --add-pairmode=1400x1050+1680x1050
Password:
FGLRX_AddPairMode failed when try to add mode : 1400x1050+1680x1050 
Using /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Saved back-up to /etc/X11/xorg.conf.fglrx-0
```

Ignore the error message, test pairmode again!
```
$ aticonfig --list-pairmode
Get pair modes : 2
 0    1680x1050 + 1680x1050 --> 3360x1050 
 1    1400x1050 + 1680x1050 --> 3080x1050 
```

:-D it's there!! aticonfig now interprets xorg.conf correctly... nice!

Now, let's watch our current effort in a separate X session WITHOUT restarting X (no CTRL ALT BACKSPACE). Go to a Terminal, eg tty2 (CTRL ALT F2) and type
```
xinit -- :2
```
This should start an additional X session in tty8 with an xterm session, grey (b+w) background and an "X" as your mouse pointer.
IF IT DOES NOT: in case 'xinit -- :2' resulted in error messages, go back to your current X session CTRL ALT F7 and check xorg.conf and the error messages in Xorg.2.log. To ensure a working xorg.conf next time, restore the original file via 'sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf.failsafe /etc/X11/xorg.conf'.

Now, back to the recently started X session on tty8 - it uses the altered version of xorg.conf.
Let's see if pairmode 1400x1050 + 1680x1050 is supported! In the xterm console do
```
$ xrandr
 SZ:    Pixels          Physical       Refresh
*0   3360 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )  *60  
 1   3080 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 2   1400 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 3   1680 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 4   1280 x 1024   ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   70   60  
 5   1280 x 768    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 6   1152 x 864    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   70   60  
 7   1024 x 768    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   72   70   60  
 8   1024 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 9    848 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 10   800 x 600    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   72   70   60   56   47  
 11   720 x 576    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 12   720 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 13   640 x 480    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   72   60  
 14   640 x 400    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   60  
 15   640 x 350    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 16   512 x 384    ( 948mm x 303mm )   60  
 17   400 x 300    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   60  
 18   320 x 240    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   60  
 19   320 x 200    ( 948mm x 303mm )   75   60  
Current rotation - normal
Current reflection - none
Rotations possible - normal 
Reflections possible - none

```

The asterix '*' marked setting is your current profile, but we observe a NEW entry: ' 1   3080 x 1050   ( 948mm x 303mm )   60' 

Now, kill tty8 (type 'exit' in xterm) and switch back to tty7 (CTRL ALT F7)

Restart X via CTRL ALT BACKSPACE, you won't observe any changes unless you set your Screen Resolution to '3080x1050' (we know it's there, xrandr told us before)

System > Preferences > Screen Resolution > 3080x1050

Done!

Here is a screenshot of my current desktop and xorg.conf file - using the correct screen resolution on both screens:
[IMG]http://austrian.n3rds.net/uploads/3080x1050.png[/IMG]

[SIZE="3"]NOTE 3: the crucial settings are marked **bold**[/SIZE]

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#
#   ********************************
#   ** BIG DESKTOP CONFIGURATION  **
#   ********************************
#
#   kEinstein	19-Jan-07


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "de"
	Option	    "XkbVariant" "nodeadkeys"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom" 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"       
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom" 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
	**Option	    "PairModes" "1400x1050+1680x1050"**
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		**Modes    "1400x1050" "1680x1050"**
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

with fglrx enabled:
```
$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: MOBILITY RADEON X300 Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6011 (8.28.8)
```

Finally, I hope this will help you in solving your issues!

---

### Post by subliminal on 2007-01-20
Cheers for the response, sadly still it isn't working.

Trying to use aticonfig to add pairmodes didnt work, it would blank the line in xorg.conf, even if I started with a fresh xorg.conf, and so aticonfig --list-pairmode always showed 0 modes.

I edited the file manually, matching it up almost identically to yours.

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
#	Option	    "Mode2"        "1280x1024"
#	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,CRT"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option      "HSync2" "64"
	Option      "VRefresh2" "60"
	Option      "PairMode" "1280x800+1280x1024"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes	  "1280x800" "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

And then started a new x session, which failed to display anything. Although this time it didnt lock my terminal visuals, so I could switch back to the one on which X worked with a single desktop. Looking at the log it has still crashed though, at the same place just after checking modelines for the primary display (laptop screen), so maybe it is something to do with modelines for the external lcd, which has no EDID data. Heres the log file.

Cheers for your help, figuring this out is beyond me, I can't really see why it isnt working. 
The only other thing I was thinking of is perhaps an xserver --reconfigure to get a totaly new xorg.conf with both monitors in, but I'm sceptical as to whether this will do anything.

### Post by batsy on 2007-01-28
Hi,  I installed Ubuntu today and first thing wanted is to get my dual monitors going.  Being new to Linux this is proving to be more challenging than I would have thought.  I did the fglrx thing and added the code for ATI big desktop and thought I did everything right, but not getting the monitors to stop mirroring themselves.  Can anyone see what I'm doing wrong?  I have a Radeon 9800 with 2 - 19" LCD's set as 1024 x 1280.

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"

EndSection

	Load  "dri"

	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"

	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"

EndSection

EndSection

EndSection

	Mode         0666

### Post by kEinstein on 2007-01-29
> **batsy said:**
> ...  I did the fglrx thing and added the code for ATI big desktop and thought I did everything right, but not getting the monitors to stop mirroring themselves.  Can anyone see what I'm doing wrong?  I have a Radeon 9800 with 2 - 19" LCD's set as 1024 x 1280.

Hi batsy,
you correctly added the settings to your xorg.conf. Unfortunately, you forgot to delete or outcomment (#) your old settings. You have both drivers enabled: 'fglrx' and 'ati'. Nevermind!
Furthermore, I think you want to have a 'BigDesktop' setting!? BigDesktop provides a desktop which spans over multiple screens. Dual-Head would also be possible in your case - see the forums for a detailed description.

THIS ought to be YOUR working xorg.conf with BigDesktop :) c/p it, keep a working copy of your old xorg.conf as backup! ```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection


# -> relevant changes start here

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option	    "Mode2" "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option	    "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

# <- relevant changes stop here


Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "0"
EndSection

### Post by danomatika on 2007-02-01
After install the fglrx binary drivers, I made a copy of my xorg.conf and ran

```
sudo aticonfig --initial=dual-head
```

I am comfortable with editing my xorg.conf, but I don't really need to as the aticonfig utility works pretty well.  Switching to tv out, turning on bigdesktop, and changing refresh rates works quite well.  Just checkout the help for more options:

```
aticonfig --help | more
```

Bigdesktop works great for my Thinkpad T43 and ATI mobility x300.  LCD and CRT for bigdesktop at the same resolution.  Windows respect the screens and I have the panel on only 1, which is what i want.  My only problem is the hardware overlay or opengl layer or whatever its called.  If I run an accelerated app like glxgears, the accelerated layer is offset off of the window to the left.  It hards to explain but a picture will help:

[IMG]http://www.robotcowboy.com/dump/Screenshot.png[/IMG]

SDL apps work fine, but anything using opengl (google earth, open arena, unreal tournery, etc) are offset like this.  Fullscreen stuff mirrors on both screens, but, once again, they layer is offset about 300 pixels or so to the left.

I noticed if I run vlc (which, i believe uses opengl for the video layer) it only works on the main monitor, i.e. a black window on the second display.  If I run vlc on the first then move my mouse to the second, the opengl layer moves to the left and, thus, the video slides to the left inside the vlc window.

Of course, in single monitor mode, everything works fine .... but it would be great to figure out whats going on.  I eradicated Billy Boy's XP from this machine over break and, so far, the dual screen setup works just as well as in windows ... except for this.

Just wondering if anyone else has experienced this.

EDIT:  Ok, I feel dumb, but right after I posted this I tried adding this line to my xorg.conf:

```
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
```

And the opengl layer now works great ... actually bigdestop is now just as good as in windows, yeah!

VLC is now the only one that is acting up, as other accelerated windows work fine on both moniters, so thats a vlc only problem then.

UPDATE: VLC and the gl overlay works perfectly after I upgraded to fglrx 8.32.5

---

### Post by Snille on 2007-02-04
Hi, I used [this](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/XGL-Ubuntu) guide to setup beryl before I read yours.
But now if I do the changes in my xorg.conf and then try to use the "XGL" session X server say: Fatal error: no screens found (after a while).

My best guess is that it has to do with the beryl setup. But I'm not sure. 
If I login normally (Gnome) and run fglrxinfo I don't get the Radeon driver loaded.

But if I change back to my original xorg.conf (before big desktop) the XGL session works... But then of course with mirrored screens...

Here's my not working xorg.conf:
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "se"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"  # Change to 
                                           # /dev/input/event
                                           # for USB
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"  # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"  # Change to 
                                           # /dev/input/event
                                           # for USB
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"  # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"  # Change to 
                                           # /dev/input/event
                                           # for USB
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"  # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option	    "Mode2"         "1600x1200" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option	    "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1600x1200"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection
```

I'm fairly new to Linux... But I'm learning. :)

---

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection
```

I'm fairly new to Linux... But I'm learning. :)

I'm afraid that the ATI's BigDesktop method does a very poor job in operating XGL+Berly with dual screens.  To my knowledge, BigDesktop doesn't support XGL yet, or that there's a resolution restriction.  In other words, your total (combined) screen resolution has to be 1024x768 or less in order to even think about using XGL on BigDesktop.  

So, good luck!

Nvidia's binary driver is much better and i have seen demonstrations of XGL on TwinView (equivalent of BigDesktop). Most of the videos that appear on the web with XGL+Dual Screen is usually done in this matter.

---

### Post by marquito on 2007-02-06
Hi,
I have to monitors (laptop internal 1024x768, external lcd 1280x1024). I use them in extended horizontal desktop mode. Everything is fine as long as I use 1024x768 on both monitors and the tips posted above.

However if I want to use 1024x768 (internal) and 1280x1024 (external) it does not work properly. At first glance I thought everthing is alright, however it turns out that the resolution on the internal monitor is not set properly. If I move the mouse down to the edge of the display (internal) and up again, some how it gets misaligned. If I move the mouse up to the top edge it is aligned again.

A closer look at delivers the problem:
using xrandr delivers: 

```

SZ:    Pixels          Physical       Refresh
 0   2560 x 1024   ( 803mm x 302mm )   60
*1   2304 x 1024   ( 803mm x 302mm )  *60
 2   1024 x 768    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   72   70   60
 3   1280 x 1024   ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   70   60   47   43
 4    800 x 600    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   72   70   60   56
 5    720 x 576    ( 803mm x 302mm )   60
 6    848 x 480    ( 803mm x 302mm )   60
 7    720 x 480    ( 803mm x 302mm )   60
 8    640 x 480    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   72   60
 9    640 x 400    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   60
 10   640 x 350    ( 803mm x 302mm )   70   60
 11   512 x 384    ( 803mm x 302mm )   60
 12   400 x 300    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   60
 13   320 x 240    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   60
 14   320 x 200    ( 803mm x 302mm )   75   60

```
 
using aticonfig --list-pairmode delivers:
```

0    1024x768 + 1280x1024 --> 2304x1024
1    1280x1024 + 1280x1024 --> 2560x1024

```

It becomes clear that the height of the internal display is set wrong. Has anybody similar problems or even any ideas how to solve this problem? 

Here an outline of the (I think) important parts of the xorg.conf:

```

Section "Device"
#   Option      "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
  Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
  Driver      "fglrx"
  Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
  Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
  Option      "HSync2" "30-81" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display.
  Option      "VRefresh2" "56-76" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
  Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
#   Option      "PairModes" "1024x768+1024x768" # this works
  Option      "PairModes" "1024x768+1280x1024"
  BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
   Identifier "Default Screen"
   Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
   Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
   DefaultDepth     24
   SubSection "Display"
     Viewport   0 0
     Depth     24
#     Modes    "1024x768" "1024x768" # this works
     Modes    "1024x768" "1280x1024"
   EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I have already tried to use the Option Mode2 and Virtual, however without success.

Thank you for any hints and advices,

marquito

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"

	# path to defoma fonts

	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"

	Load  "glx"

	Load  "vbe"

	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "WDE LCM-15v5"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"

	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"

	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup" "LCD,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "75" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV280 [Radeon 9200 PRO]"
	Monitor    "WDE LCM-15v5"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

### Post by sundown on 2007-02-07
I have a very annoying problem. 
As I said previously I use "clone" to have a proper resolution on my 19 w monitor while having the laptop display closed.
But there is sensible difference between the quality of video when playing a file on the bigger screen compared to the same file on the smaller laptop screen. On the big screen there is a visible annoying slow refresh line that occurs every time there is fast camera movement or fast action... fast switch of frames. While on the laptop display everything runs smoothly. Also when I open the lid and place the laptop screen next to the monitor I can easily compare the quality. How is it possible that the same content on the different screens is different in quality? Is there a possible way to fix this or I'm doomed watching movies normally on the big screen?

i think i entered the info correctly 

below is my xorg.conf


```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
  # path to defoma fonts
  FontPath "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
  Load "i2c"
  Load "bitmap"
  Load "ddc"
  Load "extmod"
  Load "freetype"
  Load "int10"
  Load "type1"
  Load "vbe"
  load "glx"
  load "GLcore"
  load "dri"
  load "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
  Driver "kbd"
  option "CoreKeyboard"
  option "XkbRules" "xorg"
  option "XkbModel" "pc105"
  option "XkbLayout" "gb"
  option "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Configured Mouse"
  Driver "mouse"
  option "CorePointer"
  option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
  option "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
  option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
  option "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver "wacom"
  Identifier "stylus"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "stylus"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver "wacom"
  Identifier "eraser"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "eraser"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver "wacom"
  Identifier "cursor"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "cursor"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
EndSection

Section "Device"
  identifier "Generic Video Card"
  boardname "ATI Radeon (fglrx)"
  busid "PCI:1:0:0"
  driver "ati"
  screen 0
  vendorname "ATI"
  option "MergedFB" "off"
  Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
  Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor
  Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
  Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
  Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
  Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  identifier "Generic Monitor"
  vendorname "Generic"
  modelname "Flat Panel 1280x800"
  HorizSync 31.5-90
  VertRefresh 60
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x768@60" 80.14 1280 1344 1480 1680 768 769 772 795 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1600x1024@60" 136.36 1600 1704 1872 2144 1024 1025 1028 1060 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1680x1050@60" 147.14 1680 1784 1968 2256 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1920x1200@60" 193.16 1920 2048 2256 2592 1200 1201 1204 1242 -hsync +vsync
  gamma 1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier "Default Screen"
  Device "Generic Video Card"
  Monitor "Generic Monitor"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    depth 24
    modes "1280x768@60" "1600x1024@60" "800x600@60" "1680x1050@60" "1920x1200@60"
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
  InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
  InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
  InputDevice "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
  Mode 0666
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

```

any help or tips most welcome,

thanks in advance

Subz

PS: i have beryl installed if that makes any difference?

### Post by Ziox on 2007-02-08
since you are using the "ati" driver, you need to enable the MergedFB method, which is located on one of my other thread.
However, if you wish to use Big-Desktop, you need to install the fglrx driver. (I believe that a link is included in my post)

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-03-10
Confirming another BIGDESKTOP with different screen resolutions. thnx kEistien and Ziox. {back on page 3}

The "pair mode" showed up for me when I manually edited xorg.conf, but it's still a good idea to pop over to tty*X* and "xinit -- :*X*" 
then check "aticonfig --list-paimode".

Leodp, it sounds like one of your monitor isn't DPMS'ing, don't under estimate the power of googling for your h&v sync values.

/redwheelbarrow


Here's my code, strangely similar to kEistien's :-p....

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#
#   ********************************
#   ** BIG DESKTOP CONFIGURATION  **
#   ********************************
#
#  redwheelbarrow 03-10-07


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom" 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"       
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom" 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1400x1050+1680x1050"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1400x1050" "1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection



```

EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
Mode 0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
Option "Composite" "false"
EndSection

Thanks, Tim

Thanks for any help that you can provide!

Thanks
Tim

### Post by s0urce on 2007-03-13
> **eivo said:**
> I have mine half working. I get the large desktop at the login screen after I reboot, but not after I log in.  This is what my xorg.conf looks like. 

I am really new at linux, so any help would be greatly appreciated. Thanks. :)

I had the same problem. To fix it I did the following:

System > Prefrences > Screen Resolution
Select the resolution that is a combination of both screen resloutions wide and your height.

In my case, I have 2 widescreen monitors running 1680x1050. I simply needed to select 3360x1050 as my resolution. My Xorg.conf is below:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

    # path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "no"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
	Option	    "Mode2" "1680x1050"
	BusID       "PCI:5:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection

```

### Post by s0urce on 2007-03-14
> **mdampier said:**
> I have a similar problem as eivo originally described. At the login prompt I seem to have two working screens and the pointer goes across both screens but after I log in my desktop appears fully on my left monitor and only about a fourth of  my right  monitor. That fourth of a screen for some reason contains my squished menu.

I have two Dell 2001FP Monitors and I am running a ATI Radeon X300 card with Big Desktop.

I have tried what S0urce suggest to eivo but it totally crashed my x-sever and I could only reboot in recovery mode.

Below is the xorg file that has me almost working at 1.4 monitors.

I can't seem to get the right resolution to show up in the gui to change it.

Please HELP i want to tear my hair out and I am a newb so be descriptive.

Change this in your xorg.conf
```

Option      "EnablePrivateBackZ" "no"

```
