### Post by Darell on 2007-03-21
here is my Xorg s0urce, hope you can help me!:) 

With big desktop:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
	Load  "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc11"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 51.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseFBDev" "true"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option "DesktopSetup" "CRT,CRT" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

and another method I'm currently using (dont know exactly what, I just looked at the xorg files from other people) :

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc11"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 51.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
        Option      "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
        Option      "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

Both work great in KDE but not in my XGL session 	:-(

### Post by leonya on 2007-03-22
Thank you, kEinstein!  Basically, copying your xorg.conf got my setup working.  IBM T60 with ATI  X1300.

However, I wasted at least 3 or 4 hours, trying a million different things without any luck, all because I was typing in resolution not supported by my monitor!!!  Make sure you use resolution actually supported by the monitor (as recognized by the video card)!  For example, I have a 1600x1200 external monitor, but was trying to set it to 1600x1050 (to match the 1400x1050 laptop LCD), and kept getting it reset to 1400x1050.  Only when I specified 1600x1200 did ATI accept that mode!

### Post by mikko.ohtamaa on 2007-03-25
I just tried ATI Big Desktop and Feisty.

After four hours of struggling it worked. I have HP nx9420 laptop with ATI x1600 mobile Radeon display card. Laptop internal screen is 1680x1050 and the external monitor is 1280x1024.

When I first run aticonfig --dtop=horizontal, aticonfig wanted to force 1024x768 on both screens. I tried every tip of manual Xorg.conf hacking: Virtual screen span, setting up fake resolutions. Tens of reboots. Finally I was ready gave up. 

Then I run aticonfig --force --initial and aticonfig --dtop=horizontal once more. Suddenly it started to work. Both screens were in native resolution and I could drag windows from a screen to another.

aticonfig --list-pairmode reveals
```

root@hacker:~# aticonfig --list-pairmode
Get pair modes : 2
 0    1680x1050 + 1680x1050 --> 3360x1050 
```

I can move my mouse cursor off the external monitor. Windows maximize themselves correctly on the external monitor, though.

I still try to play around with aticonfig --add-pairmore to get everything working perfectly.

---

### Post by mikko.ohtamaa on 2007-03-25
Ok, 

Horizontal span is fixed now with the following command

aticonfig --add-pairmore 1650x1080+1280x1024

and changing  screen resolution using Gnome Screen Resolution tool after that.

I no longed can move mouse cursor off the right edge of my external screen. However, I can move it over the bottom edge of my external monitor. This is very annoying when you try to click items in the taskbar. Looks like nothing can cure this problem.

I created a[ blog entry about the sad state of Linux multi monitor set-up]("http://www.redinnovation.com/blog/archive/2007/03/25/dual-head-issues-with-linux")

---

01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc Radeon R350 [Radeon 9800 Pro]
01:00.1 Display controller: ATI Technologies Inc Radeon R350 [Radeon 9800 Pro] (Secondary)

### Post by tja on 2007-04-05
thx to alle the posters here i have at least a dual head setup with two distinct screens running. cheers :)
i even got big-desktop running - but my monitors seem to forbid me a nice and ergonomical way to do this.

my configuration:
- ati x-800xl with
- 22" CRT (original resolution 1600x1200@85) as primary screen (as seen from gfx) and
- 20" TFT connected via DVI (resolution is 1680x1050)

as said before atm i have it configured as two screens. funnily even if the gfx thinks the crt is first ...
```
sudo aticonfig --force --initial=dual-head --screen-layout=right
```
... built a xorg.conf where the TFT is :0.0 and the CRT is :0.1.
after that the TFT droves its native resolution, and the CRT drove 1680x1050@60.
i changed the secondary (=CRT) monitor defs to use a higher refresh rate ...
```
Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
        [b]HorizSync    28.0 - 160.0
        VertRefresh  43.0 - 100.0[/b]
EndSection
```
... and fixed it to the desired resolution ...
```
Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                **Modes "1600x1200"**
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection
```
... via adding the mode-line above.
this will give me two screens with the desired resolutions and refresh-rates. so the hardware is able to do this ...

going big-desktop im stuck.
every possible attempt to get big-desktop running will result in one of the following:

nearest to optimal:
tried:
"pairing" to "1600x1200+1680x1050" with the "virtual"-trick and removing the "HorizSync" and "VertRefresh" statements for the CRT
result:
big-desktop works with the desired resolutions but both monitors will refresh at 60Hz - which is a pain to work and not acceptable.

unuseable:
tried:
let "HorizSync" and "VertRefresh" stay for the CRT and fooling around with HSync2, VertRefresh2 and/or trying to give "aticonfig-Monitor[0]" his own "HorizSync" and "VertRefresh" statements
result:
TFT is out of sync (didnt know that its possible to bring a DVI-connected screen out of sync ...) and CRT **is still on 60Hz with all different configurations !**

to make it clear: even if i change the sync-ranges for the CRT and TFT is out of sync the CRT will stay at 60Hz.

so my final question:
has **anybody** successfully got working big-desktop with different resolutions **and different refresh-rates** ?

PS: i thougth i could live with two screens and without big-desktop - but then i tried to start firefox on the second screen - "firefox is already running, but its not responding blah blah blah" :(

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-04-05
> so my final question:
has **anybody** successfully got working big-desktop with different resolutions **and different refresh-rates** ?


No, but you should be able to find a middle ground with your monitor section and VRefresh2, Hsync2. (say 70hz?)
Since big desktop is actually just one screen according to X, I don't think it's possible.

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
[B]	Option	    "HSync2" "30-82"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "56-76"[/B]
	Option	    "PairModes" "1680x1050+1400x1050"
#	Option	    "PBuffer" "4"
#	Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"

	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by arthur24b6 on 2007-04-06

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-04-06
> **tja said:**
> no luck, but thanks for your effort.
it seems there is no way to force the tft to drive at 70Hz - i even connected it via analoge cable instead of DVI - but some automatic in the fglrx driver still persists to use 60Hz.
in the log i found some TMDS thing - tried to turn it off via "--force-monitor=notmds1,notmds2" but it changed nothing.
have to ebay that 22" CRT - but im sure i will miss it :(

I haven't tried this personally but...

You could add the ignoreEDID option to your device section.

[I]Option "IgnoreEDID" "boolean"

    Do not use EDID data for mode validation, but DDC is still used for monitor detection. This is different from Option "NoDDC". The default is: "off".

    If the server is ignoring your modlines, set this option to "on" and try again.[/I]

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-04-06
> **arthur24b6 said:**
> I followed these instructions on my machine (kubuntu, x1300) and wasn't ever able to get it to move out of "clone" mode. I noticed that my login screen wasn't cloned and when I logged in in single user and did startx, it would work. I moved my .kde file to .kde.old and suddenly everything was working. Of course, I lost all my preferences, but it seems that maybe kde is saving some data that prevents big desktop? not sure but, it's worth trying if you're banging your head.

THANKYOU THANKYOU THANKYOU!! That's all I needed to know.

To fix the default screen resolution for kde you'll have to manually edit /home/*username*/.kde/share/config/displayconfigrc

Change width to your desired pixel width, but make sure X can support it (ie. there exists a matching xrandr entity)


For example here's my displayconfigrc for 3080X1050:

```
[General]
targetgamma=2.0

[Screen0]
dpmsEnabled=on
dpmsSeconds=2700
height=1050
reflectX=0
reflectY=0
refresh=60
rotate=0
width=3080

```


This is an ugly hack, and I'd love to be able to set the resolution through system settings.
When you change resolutions through system settings kde writes to /home/*username*/.kde/share/config/systemsettingsrc

mine looks like this:
```
[Colors]
CurrentPalette=

[MainWindow]
Height 1050=795
Height 1200=788
Height 1344=780
Height 400=401
Height 480=481
Height 768=558
Width 1024=810
Width 1400=810
Width 1600=1194
Width 1680=975
Width 1792=810
Width 640=641

```

Each height and width correspond to a mode that I can set through system settings, and I have no idea what the *=XXX* means...

any Ideas?

---

### Post by tja on 2007-04-11
> **redwheelbarrow said:**
> I haven't tried this personally but...

You could add the ignoreEDID option to your device section.

[I]Option "IgnoreEDID" "boolean"

    Do not use EDID data for mode validation, but DDC is still used for monitor detection. This is different from Option "NoDDC". The default is: "off".

    If the server is ignoring your modlines, set this option to "on" and try again.[/I]
yeah, thx - that did the trick for me, at least partially ;) 

at least i could get 75Hz on both screens, adding the infamous "Virtual" to it made it even to 1600x1200 on the CRT.
the downside is that the TFT now wouldnt let me get more then 1280x1024 - stretched and interpolated. even adding some carefully calculated modelines would not do it - he just ignores it :(

but (just FYI if somebody has a compareable configuration of TFT/CRT as i have) im switching to Xinerama for now. as you have two distinct screen/monitor configs there you can specify the monitors parameters as you like and it works (ok, no 3d and i have to use SWCursor but thats ok for /me).

maybe the drivers big-desktop mode will allow it for me in the future or i really get rid of the CRT, then im back here again :)

### Post by earthmeLon on 2007-04-14
I was having trouble with my wine/opengl rendering only 3/4ths of the screen.  I figured out that the cause of this problem was my second monitor's resolution wasn't set up properly.  My second monitor had a thick black bar on the right hand side. 

Make sure you scroll down and read the author of this thread's post on how to fix this problem to get opengl to work properly!

```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "eView 17f2"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AR [Radeon 9600 XT]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AR [Radeon 9600 XT]"
	Monitor    "eView 17f2"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

```

Uhm, Looks like you're missing a lot under your device section.  This is what mine looks like:

```

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
	Option	    "PairMode" "1440x900+1280x1024" # Pair mode resolutions
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

```

### Post by unibuntu on 2007-04-16
> **unibuntu said:**
> Mikko, I have the X1650 and the same problems with main monitor 1440x900 and second 1280x1024
I can get both monitors to work fine but no big-desktop just two seperate sessions on two monitors.
Unfortunately your link won't let me see your blog, so can you post/send me a copy of your xorg.conf pease?
Thanks

I have it running now!
Thanks to ZIOX, Keinstein et.al.
The thing that was wrong was that ATI config created two monitor entries.
So reverted back to one monitor entry only.
Set all the settings for the seccond screen nusing the parameters with the 2 at the end
The using "Virtual" to set the resolution 1440 x 1024 (I use a 1440x900 plus a 1280x124 monitor)
 And now comes the big mistery

To actually get it to work I must log in and use the preferences->screen resolution to set the virtual resolution!
This was the bit of info I was missing before so all the xorg.conf settings made no impact until I did this.
Now all is well.

So next I will reduce to 1280 x 1024 and then try Beryl...

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-04-16
You have two screen sections and well two of everything:-p

It probably doesn't make a difference since your layout calls the correct screen but try this xorg.conf if you're brave. MAKE SURE YOU BACKUP YOUR SEMI-WORKING XORG.CONF, since it's can always get worse.

The key take aways from the conf below are:
1. Cleanliness, delete those orphaned sections
2. Define a range for Hsync and Vref (and Hsync2 and Vref2). YOU CAN EVEN GOOGLE FOR THE SUPPORTED RANGE OF YOUR MONITOR


Also you might have to set you resolution once you get into gnome or kde.


```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#
#   ********************************
#   ** BIG DESKTOP CONFIGURATION  **
#   ********************************
#
#  redwheelbarrow 04-16-07


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
#	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
#	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
#	Driver      "synaptics"
#	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
#	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
#	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
#	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
#EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier  "stylus"
#	Driver      "wacom"
#	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"
#	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
#	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"
#EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier  "eraser"
#	Driver      "wacom"
#	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom" 
#	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
#	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"       
#EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier  "cursor"
#	Driver      "wacom"
#	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom" 
#	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
#	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"
#EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
	HorizSync   30-82
	VertRefresh 52-85
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "30-82"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "52-85"
#	Option	    "PairModes" "H1xV1+H2xV2"
#	Option	    "PBuffer" "4"
	Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "disable"
EndSection


```

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-04-18
The first one isn't even using the fglrx driver, that should be a hint:-p

Also you can read the layout section, and see which screen it calls, then see what device is called in the screen.


```
Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "30-82"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "52-85"
#	Option	    "PairModes" "H1xV1+H2xV2"
#	Option	    "PBuffer" "4"
	Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"
EndSection

```


> **RunJun said:**

```
Section "Device"

	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"

	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"

### Post by zabbaskeema on 2007-04-25
Hi There, 

I have a working setup, Laptop on the right, LCD on the left, big screen and fglrx latest (2.0.6458 (8.36.5)).

Everything would be perfect but for two things:

1-) **I cant move any icons to the secondary screen.[COLOR="Red"][/COLOR]**  (Application windows are fine).  The icon would drag, but not drop in the 2nd monitor.

2-) glxgear gives me only about 150 FPS (but fgl_glxgears gives me 400 FPS)
I get this in the Xorg.0.log:

[COLOR="Red"](EE) AIGLX error: dlsym for __driCreateNewScreen_20050727 failed (/usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so: undefined symbol: __driCreateNewScreen_20050727)
(EE) AIGLX: reverting to software rendering[/COLOR]

Attacehd is my xorg.conf

Anyone has a clue on these?

Cheers!

Pat

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-04-25
> **zabbaskeema said:**
> Hi There, 

I have a working setup, Laptop on the right, LCD on the left, big screen and fglrx latest (2.0.6458 (8.36.5)).

Everything would be perfect but for two things:

1-) I cant move any icons to the secondary screen.  (Application windows are fine).  The icon wuold drag, but not drop in the 2nd monitor.

2-) glxgear gives me only about 150 FPS (but fgl_glxgears gives me 400 FPS)
I get this in the Xorg.0.log:

(EE) AIGLX error: dlsym for __driCreateNewScreen_20050727 failed (/usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so: undefined symbol: __driCreateNewScreen_20050727)
(EE) AIGLX: reverting to software rendering

Attacehd is my xorg.conf

Anyone has a clue on these?

Cheers!

Pat

1 No cure for the icons as of yet. 

2 For AIGLW, make sure usr/lib/libGL.so.1 is a link to /usr/lib/xorg/libGL.so.1.2.  Or you might have /usr/lib/ati-fglrx/libGL.so.1.2... run a locate libGL.so.1
(or even better install fglrx 8.34.8 from the repository). 
Run fglrxinfo to see what you're using now.

Then you'll need to turn video overlay off, and opengl overlay on:

Option	    "VideoOverlay" "off"
Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "on"


Here's mine:
```
Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "30-82"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "52-85"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1680x1050+1400x1050"
	Option	    "PBuffer" "4"
	Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by zabbaskeema on 2007-04-25
Hi there!

Option "VideoOverlay" "off"
 Option "OpenGLOverlay" "on"

Fixed it for glxgear!

root@zabba:/home/zabba# glxgears
11541 frames in 5.0 seconds = 2308.153 FPS
13846 frames in 5.1 seconds = 2713.788 FPS
20178 frames in 5.0 seconds = 4035.548 FPS
28133 frames in 5.0 seconds = 5626.512 FPS
12616 frames in 5.0 seconds = 2522.777 FPS

Nice!

The icon stuff is a bugger... :-(

Thanks a lot!

---

	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection


---

### Post by sssp on 2007-05-03
Is it possible too have two different resolutions?
One monitor is 1680x1050 and the other is 1280x1024. Right now the resolution is 2560x1024 so it looks funny on the widescreen monitor. I am using fglrx drivers ATI 8.35.5 which I used envy to install. This is on Feisty.


```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 51.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies Inc Radeon R350 [Radeon 9800 Pro]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option "Mode2"         "1680x1050" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is 		connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the 		secondary display. 
	Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary 		display.
	Option "PairMode" "1680x1050+1280x1024" # Pair mode resolutions
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies Inc Radeon R350 [Radeon 9800 Pro]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

### Post by styson on 2007-05-04
I too need two separate resolutions.  My 17" LCD is on the left, 1280x1024, my laptop is on the right (1680x1050).  I have a ATI X700 card, using 8.34 version of the ATI driver and I'm running ubuntu 6.10.  I have successfully (not using this guide but instructions cobbled together off the net) created an xorg.conf file that gives me two separete desktops.  The mouse moves between the two but I cannot move windows between the two.  So I have to launch apps on the appropriate workspace and some apps don't behave nicely like OO which ends up on my  main screen (laptop) regardless of where I launched it.  

What I'm after is the way XP does dual head.  One big desktop with each monitor running at it's normal resolution.  Is this possible?

Here is my xorg.conf file.  I pretty much created everything but the input devices from scratch.  Not sure if it's "correct" but it does work.  All feedback is welcome.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Dual"
	Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
	Screen      1  "Screen1" LeftOf "Screen0"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	#Load  "extmod"
	SubSection "extmod"
    	    Option "omit xfree86-dga"
  	EndSubSection
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "false"
	Option	    "Buttons" "7"
	Option	    "ButtonMapping" "1 2 3 6 7"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Card0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VendorName"  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Screen 	    0
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Card1"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VendorName"  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Screen 	    1
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier  "LCD"
	Option	    "VendorName"  "Asus"
	Option	    "ModelName"   "PowerPro15:4"
	Option	    "DPMS" 
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier "Xerox XG-71D"
	Option	    "VendorName"  "Xerox"
	Option      "ModelName"   "XG-71D"
#	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen0"
	Device     "Card0"
	Monitor    "LCD"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes    "1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen1"
	Device     "Card1"
	Monitor    "Xerox XG-71D"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "0"
EndSection
```

### Post by styson on 2007-05-04
I answered my own question.  DO'H!  By removing all references to the second screen and card, I was able to get a big desktop working.    Although my screen resolution is physically larger than my two monitors can display (2960x1050) when I use maximize for a window, it respects the defined monitor resolution so it's workable.

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-05-06
> **styson said:**
> I answered my own question.  DO'H!  By removing all references to the second screen and card, I was able to get a big desktop working.    Although my screen resolution is physically larger than my two monitors can display (2960x1050) when I use maximize for a window, it respects the defined monitor resolution so it's workable.

You should look back a few pages, several people have done this with already using paimodes.
Notably first by Keinstien on **page 3**.



```

Section "Device"	
        Identifier  "Card0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "30-82"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "52-85"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1680x1050+1280x1050"
	Option	    "PBuffer" "4"
	Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"
#	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection


```

---

### Post by styson on 2007-05-06
Yes, I used that information (thanks for the hints) to get my two monitors setup properly.  
I do have to change me desktop resoultion back to 1680x1050 when I undock my laptop, but besides that it works as expected, and pretty much exactly as XP does.

### Post by wyth on 2007-05-07
This will hopefully be a dead-simple question.

I have everything set up just fine, but am looking for a way to turn ATI Big-Desktop off on the fly.

I tried aticonfig --enable-monitor=lvds --effective=now ([from here]("http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Feisty_Installation_Guide#Additional_configure_with_aticonfig_tool")), but that didn't do anything (that I know of); my external monitor was disconnected from my laptop, but my laptop still showed big desktops, so some apps were opening off-screen and making things difficult.

Is there an easy way to do it short of commenting out the options in my xorg.conf and restarting X?  That seems a bit much.

### Post by tarjei on 2007-05-07
The easiest solution is to us System -> Preferences -> Screen Resolution and change it to the resolution of your laptop monitor.

### Post by ignignokt00 on 2007-05-11
I normally use an XGL session with Beryl (ATI x800).  Trying what's in the first post seems to work in the login screen fine, but it just shows the orange screen for a few moments then X restarts and I get the login again.  Help?

### Post by Ziox on 2007-05-12
> **ignignokt00 said:**
> I normally use an XGL session with Beryl (ATI x800).  Trying what's in the first post seems to work in the login screen fine, but it just shows the orange screen for a few moments then X restarts and I get the login again.  Help?

I forgot to mention.  Composite on dual monitors DOES NOT work on ATI cards.  Nvidia card users can enable dual monitor and composite quite easily.  However, for ATI, the combined resolution of both monitors must be smaller than 2048x768 (if I remember correctly). <= I was able to achieve this, but the composite layer was acting all wacky.

### Post by cypherl0k on 2007-05-15
I had the same problem you did Arrrrgh.
I'd re-run through the ATI config step.  I noticed that it wasn't until I followed the steps below from another thread,  that my xorg.conf
changed to show as an ATI card and "generic autodetecting monitor".

```

sudo apt-get install xorg-driver-fglrx

sudo depmod -a

sudo aticonfig --initial 
sudo aticonfig --overlay-type=Xv

```

Once that was set - I could then change the resolutions as needed.
And then using Ziox's info from the first post, adding in the code he posted.
But here's my conf from monitor section down.
```

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
        Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
        Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
        Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
        Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display.
        Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "0"
EndSection

                                                                                                                                          144,0-1       Bot


```



I'm not saying that's the right way - but it's what I did and it worked :lolflag:

---

### Post by c4r1 on 2007-05-15
Hi all

I use a laptop and a lcd monitor plugged in.
Connected monitors: lvds, tmds2

### Post by vdobriakov on 2007-05-20
Every couple of weeks I try to make my ThinkPad notebook and its ATI graphic card doing exactly what I want. It is almost ;-) working...

Some comments, which (I hope) will help others interpreting other howtos:

1.The first post in this thread possibly has a typo: the option "DesktopSetup" mentioned twice. The second one should probably be "MonitorLayout".

2.Configuration of ATI's fglrx is a moving target: different versions have different settings and it seems like the meaning changes from version to version as well. So do not wonder, if howtos from others do not work for you.

3.There were a lot of questions in this forum regarding setting different resolution for two monitors in the "big desktop" mode. From the documentation by ATI (/usr/share/doc/fglrx/articles/4485.html)  >  Big Desktop [snip][INDENT]Both monitors have to operate with the same video mode settings [/INDENT] So it is not possible. You can try "dual head" mode instead, then you have independent screen and monitor sections in your xorg.conf

4.It seems safer not to edit xorg.conf and do no copy and paste from the howtos, but to use ```
sudo aticonfig
``` with appropriate options for editing. This way you make sure, that options are valid for the current fglrx version.

5.Tip: you can use aticonfig --enable-monitor=XXX to switch monitors on and off on the fly. No X restart needed.

6.There are four completely different modes in fglrx (single head, clone, big desktop and dual head). Depending on selected mode most of the other settings do not make any sense. For example in "big desktop" you can not select parameters for the second monitor anyway.

7.ATI support for linux is really bad. fglrx really sucks. Open source radeon driver does not have 3d acceleration for my Mobility X300. AMD! Please change this!

Good luck!

---

### Post by colonelk on 2007-05-22
I'm almost there but it seems either X isn't at all clever or ATI's drivers aren't at all clever when swapping between a docked and undocked profile.

Basically I configured Big Dekstop using the Catalyst control Center and it worked fine.  I then undocked, tried the machine and again it seemed fine at the laptops native res of 1440x1050.  I then redocked and switched on again and now I'm back in cloned mode.

Am I going to have to select Big Desktop EVERY time I change from an undocked to a docked Config?

In Vista and XP I can "hot" undock and it'll reconfigure the resolutions perfectly.  I can then "Hot" dock and it'll pick up the two external screens perfectly and change resolution to suit.   WHY ISN'T IT THIS SIMPLE IN Feisty!!!   ARRRGRGGGGH!!! ;)

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-05-22
> **vdobriakov said:**
> Every couple of weeks I try to make my ThinkPad notebook and its ATI graphic card doing exactly what I want. It is almost ;-) working...

Some comments, which (I hope) will help others interpreting other howtos:
3.There were a lot of questions in this forum regarding setting different resolution for two monitors in the "big desktop" mode. From the documentation by ATI (/usr/share/doc/fglrx/articles/4485.html)   So it is not possible. You can try "dual head" mode instead, then you have independent screen and monitor sections in your xorg.conf

6.There are four completely different modes in fglrx (single head, clone, big desktop and dual head). Depending on selected mode most of the other settings do not make any sense. For example in "big desktop" you can not select parameters for the second monitor anyway.
Good luck!
Thanks for the tips... **BUT**
You're a little confused on the meaning of the documentation on 3. 
> *"Both monitors have to operate with the same video mode settings and only one window manager can be used."*

This means the screens share the same video configuration (overlay,Pbuffer, religion, device section, zip code , etc), however 
glitter---> **_DIFFERENT RESOLUTIONS ARE SUPPORTED WITH BIG DESKTOP MODE_**. <--- neon lights.
ie. the unified Frame buffer can be rationed out to either screen anyway you like.

and onto #6, 
Some driver options are include to do just that (control parameters on other monitors)... 
For example, my personal favorite;
> Option "VRefresh2" "frequency"
    Vertical refresh rate range for the second monitor in e.g. big desktop mode.

Wether or not this actually works is debatable, but the intent was...


Watch the misinformation please.

---

### Post by redwheelbarrow on 2007-05-22
> **colonelk said:**
> I'm almost there but it seems either X isn't at all clever or ATI's drivers aren't at all clever when swapping between a docked and undocked profile.

Basically I configured Big Dekstop using the Catalyst control Center and it worked fine.  I then undocked, tried the machine and again it seemed fine at the laptops native res of 1440x1050.  I then redocked and switched on again and now I'm back in cloned mode.

Am I going to have to select Big Desktop EVERY time I change from an undocked to a docked Config?

In Vista and XP I can "hot" undock and it'll reconfigure the resolutions perfectly.  I can then "Hot" dock and it'll pick up the two external screens perfectly and change resolution to suit.   WHY ISN'T IT THIS SIMPLE IN Feisty!!!   ARRRGRGGGGH!!! ;)

Wax on:
```
$ aticonfig --enable-monitor=lvds,crt1 --effective=now
```
Wax off:
```
$ aticonfig --enable-monitor=lvds --effective=now
```

I have *no* idea if this one will work, but if it does it'd be cool. Try it!
```
$ aticonfig --enable=auto
```

Checkout: [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Configuring]("http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Configuring")
And for those who don't like to scroll: [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/FeatureDisplaySwitching]("http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/FeatureDisplaySwitching")

### Post by forth on 2007-05-27
:KS:KS:KS:KS:KS
used your aticonfig method, works a treat although you failed to mention at the end that after the reboot sometimes the login screen looks fine but then it goes back to mirroring after login, I know a few people have reported this error and I found the answer further on in the post, basically you have to log in and set the resolution for instance when I was in mirror mode, each screen was 1024x768, after running the aticonfig method above I get a new resolution 2048x768 in my gui options in System > Preferences > Screen resolution, (the new resolution is a combined total resolution of the two screens - making a bigdesktop!).

Also you'll notice that if you had a 1024x768 (or whatever resolution of a single montor you had before) background image it can get distorted when you make the bigdesktop change, this can be rectified by either setting the background picture to "tiled" in stead of stretched, or finding a nice image that was custome made for your dual resolution, eg in my case, 2048x768. You can fine plenty of good ones on digitalblasphemy.com - although he does charge, they are worth it!

Did not need to manually edit the xorg.conf file at all, except to say that I took a backup of it:

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.bigdesktop

Thanks again! Now I can work in openoffice and watch movies again - at the same time* !!

*without using beryl transparency!

:popcorn:

---

### Post by Lovecraft on 2007-05-29
> **ignignokt00 said:**
> So there's no way to get ATI + dual monitors + Xgl/Beryl?

Following the tips on this post (thanks to all who posted), I just got my Asus V1Jp with ATI X1700 to work with dual monitors + Xgl/Beryl
Monitor 1 is the internal laptop screen, 1680x1050, second monitor is an external LCD, connected via DVI, 1280x1024 

Only problem is on second monitor, resolution is not enough to show the bottom bar, so I cannot see the minimized applications there, but if I place the mouse pointer I still can see the thumbnails (provided by Beryl), so it's not such a big deal. 

Here's the relevant parts of my xorg.conf in case someone find it useful

```
Section "Device"
    Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
    Driver      "fglrx"
    Option        "VideoOverlay" "on"
    Option        "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
    # --- The following was added for dual-head support:
    Option        "DesktopSetup" "horizontal" # Enable Big Desktop
    Option        "PairModes" "1680x1050+1280x1024" # Pair mode resolutions
    Option        "HSync2" "65.29" #The horizontal sync for the secondary display.
    Option        "VRefresh2" "75" #The refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
    Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
    Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
    DefaultDepth     24
    SubSection "Display"
        Viewport   0 0
        Depth     24
        Modes    "1680x1050" "1280x1024"
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```

### Post by mikko.ohtamaa on 2007-06-05
Hi RoboMonkey,

xorg.conf editing requires root privileges. Use sudo to run command.

max@Monkey-v2:~$ sudo /usr/X11R6/bin/aticonfig --initial

Cheers,
Mikko Ohtamaa
Oulu, Finland

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

	# path to defoma fonts

	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	HorizSync    28.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

Section "Device"

> Firefox is already running, but is not responding. To open a new window, you must first close the existing process, or restart your system.

It seems that any single screen use a personal instance of gnome, this it's going to be confirmed by the different appeal of the gadgets on the panels on the two screens (by the way they share the wallpaper, at least!).

### Post by Hasen_A on 2007-06-15
Your best bet with ATI for a dual screen is the following using only one xscreen:
```
########### DualScreen with 2 LCDs ##########################
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal,reverse" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 31-61
	Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display. 56-75
	#Option	  	"ForceMonitors" "crt1,tmds1,notv"	#Doesn't really work, use "EnableMonitor" instead
	Option	    "EnableMonitor" "notv,tmds1,crt1"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1280x1024+1024x768"
#############################################################
```

If you set up two xscreens you won't be able to move windows between the two!

---

### Post by Timorg on 2007-06-16
I am using 6.1 and I am having problems setting up my radeon 9800.

Dual monitors work with big desktop at 3840x1440@60hz, but when I try to set it up so that i have them both at 1280x960@85hz, I run into problems, both monitors are capable of doing the resolution (they are both philips, one is a 109P4 and the other is a 109B4)

I have added a pair setting for it

```
Option      "PairModes" "0x0+0x0,1280x960+1280x960"
```

but when i select the resolution by
System>Preferences>Screen Resolution i get the following result

[IMG]http://www.timorg.net/screenshot.png[/IMG]

Both browsers are maximized to each screen, on the right monitor i can move down and out of the screen into the extra space at the bottom and side, on the left monitor the mouse stays in the screen area, but if i click on any window on that monitor, my mouse is drawn higher on the screen than where the mouse focus actually is. My current understanding is that I don't really understand what is going on, I read all the posts but people who had problems getting it working, all seem to want to use a different resolution for each monitor, where I want to use the same for each. Am I supposed to have multiple "Screen" sections? or just one and the pair command? Something was said about the offset thing is a bug, I hope it goes away when the extra screen area problem is solved. The solution might have something to do with the virtual option, but that might only work if you want to enlarge the area off the screen, rather than shrink it.

I followed these [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide]("http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide") instructions to install the driver (I used the Ubuntu way) and tried a couple of the varifying things from the faq and the fglrx is installed properly

Then I followed the instructions in this thread to activate the Big-Desktop, which worked fine, except for the resolution.

Any suggestions at this point would be useful, I think I have given all the relevent information, if you need anything else just ask.

If it wasn't for the resolution issue, this would have worked 1st shot. :(

Edit:
Well I got out of bed this afternoon and decided to try something that my brain came up with in my sleep, I tried to set the monitors to a mode that was listed in xrandr, and it worked. Just because my monitors can do a resolution, doesnt mean that the video card driver can. I feel like a bit of a goose now.

Now I need to work out how to set the vertical refresh rate. I am going to try some things from sloggerkhan's post below this one. If someone could explain how to set the refresh rate (it could already be set right.

### Post by Hasen_A on 2007-06-17
[LIST=1]
[*]Install the fglrx driver using the SPM and change the xorg.conf:
Section "Device"
Driver      "fglrx"

[*]The following screen section is wrong:

```
Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "3360x1050" "2560x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```
it should look more like this, with the normal resolution of your main screen:
```
Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
```


[*]Try adding **Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor** instead,as can be seen in my section "device":
```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 (R430 UO)"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
########### DualScreen with 2 LCDs ##########################
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal,reverse" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 31-61
	Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display. 56-75
		Option	    "EnableMonitor" "notv,tmds1,crt1" # "EnableMonitor" Option	  	works better than "ForceMonitors" "
	Option	    "PairModes" "1280x1024+1024x768"
#############################################################
EndSection
```
I'm not sure if another 2nd screen section is necessary for the second monitor but it works for me. Add one just in case. Here's my xorg for studying:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"

	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"de"
	Option		"XkbVariant"	"nodeadkeys"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Buttons"		"7"
	#Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ButtonMapping"		"1 2 3 6 7"
	#Option 		"Resolution"		"100"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

  Identifier    "cursor"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 (R430 UO)"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
########### DualScreen with 2 LCDs ##########################
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal,reverse" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 31-61
	Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display. 56-75
	#Option	  	"ForceMonitors" "crt1,tmds1,notv"	#Doesn't really work, use "EnableMonitor" instead
	Option	    "EnableMonitor" "notv,tmds1,crt1"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1280x1024+1024x768"
#############################################################
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"P19-1A"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	31-64
	VertRefresh	56-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"

		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"

	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
