### Post by bitumen on 2007-06-27
hello helpful people 
System > Preferences > Screen resolution > Make Default checkbox
plays a large roll in setting the screen resolutions **after** login screen 
took me an hour to workout the /etc/X11/xorg.conf file to use big monitor and 5 hours to work out that you must set the default checkbox well as the big monitor resolutions. I was using > xrandr -s 0
 to set the resolution Screen but that dose not make the screen default(i.e. each login you have to reset the screen in terminal)  my default Screen setting for big monitor is 2048x768 hope his help some of you :p

**Big Monitor** (one desktop spanned across two monitors, can drag open files between each monitor)
[HTML]# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier     "Default Layout"
        Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
        InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
         Option "AIGLX" "on"
EndSection

Section "Files"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
        FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load  "i2c"
        Load  "bitmap"
        Load  "ddc"
        Load  "dri"
        Load  "extmod"
        Load  "freetype"
        Load  "glx"
        Load  "int10"
        Load  "type1"
        Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver      "synaptics"
        Option      "SendCoreEvents" "true"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/psaux"
        Option      "Protocol" "auto-dev"
        Option      "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
        Identifier  "stylus"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
        Option      "Type" "stylus"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
        Identifier  "eraser"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
        Option      "Type" "eraser"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
        Identifier  "cursor"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
        Option      "Type" "cursor"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
	HorizSync    60
	VertRefresh  60
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "HSync2" "60" 			#This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
        Option      "VRefresh2" "60" 		#This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
        **Option      "Mode2" "1024x768"     #can change**
        Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"		 #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
               ** Virtual   1024 768       # can change**
                Viewport  0 0
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection[/HTML]


i also have a working **Dual Head monitor** ( one desktop **on each** monitor each with its own menu, open files are lock to to their respective monitors. File links can be dragged from monitor to monitor   

[HTML]# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	Screen         "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]" #monitor position
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "on"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
 
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"   #Main monitor on the right
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	HorizSync    60
	VertRefresh  60
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"  #Secondary monitor on the left
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "HSync2" "30-82" 			#This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "56-76" 		#This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "Mode2" "1024x768"
	Option	    "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,AUTO"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"		 #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Virtual   1024 768
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection[/HTML]

**Interesting Note:** dual head config supose to be 1024 x 768 (for both monitors)
actual result 1280 x 1024 left monitor[1] and 1024 x 768 monitor [0]

included both configurations so people if they wish can compare

i'm running intel 945 gnt mobo with single 2.6 ghz Cpu and Radeon x300se 256 mb Graphic 
card

I wish to thank Zion and other people who helped on this forum, Cool work 

ps. if you find an attachment with this post its the big monitor file as a txt.file (very newbe, only been using Ubuntu 7.04 for 24 hours)

### Post by SculptusPoe on 2007-06-29
Your walk-through for the ATI Big-Desktop worked first try with only one major problem so far.

I am running dual CRTs with a Radeon X1050

Everything works fine until I try to run a program like Neverball , then the display runs out of the program window. This seems to happen with any 3D game. If I switch back to clone the display works again. 

I wouldn't mind so much except that there is no fast way to turn on/off the display option. I can disable a single monitor and turn it back on. But that doesn't fix the problem.

Is there any way to get the DesktopSetup to switch between horizontal and clone on the fly? It doesn't appear so.

[EXAMPLE_PIC]("http://server6.theimagehosting.com/image.php?img=Screenshot-Neverball.png")







Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
	Option	    "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
	Option	    "PairModes" "0x0+0x0"
	Option	    "EnableMonitor" "crt1,crt2"
	Option	    "ForceMonitors" "crt1,crt2"
EndSection

### Post by SculptusPoe on 2007-06-30
I stuck 
```

alias DSoff='sudo aticonfig --desktop-setup=single --nobackup'
alias DSon='sudo aticonfig --desktop-setup=horizontal,reverse --nobackup'

```
in .bashrc and that works quickly enough for now after a {ctrl alt <--}

                                                      # /dev/input/event

                                                      # /dev/input/event

                                                      # /dev/input/event

	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier  "Monitor[0]"

	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"

	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"

        Option 	"PairMode" 	"1600x1200+1600x1200"
	Option "DesktopSetup" "AUTO,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "no" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option "HSync2" "86" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option "VRefresh2" "180" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen[0]"
	Device     "Device[0]"

	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Virtual 1600 1200
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"

EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

```


Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "MX70"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"

### Post by irv.butler on 2007-07-04
Let me start by saying I'm a noob to linux and just deleted the windows partion today, but I have been building and programing computers since we used punch cards. I'm bragging just old lol. 


I'm using Fiesty on an adm 2400 with an all in wonder 128 I have both monitors working, but did not do it the way the how to said.  What I did was down load the driver from ati and used this command in terminal

sudo sh ati-driver-installer-8.38.6-x86.x86_64.run

then went to  System>Administration>restricted dirvers manager.   then enabled the driver and disabled the old one.

My question is can I follow this how to  or is there something else I need to do?

### Post by Praline on 2007-07-08
For those who have 2 black screens try with :
[INDENT]Option	    "Mode2" "1280x1024"[/INDENT]
instead of :
[INDENT]Option 	"PairMode" 	"...x...+...x..."[/INDENT]

Here the xorg.conf for my X800XL :
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "fr"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
#	HorizSync    28.0 - 64.0
#	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Identifier   "L70S"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
	Option	    "Mode2" "1280x1024"
#	Option	    "EnableMonitor" "crt1,crt2"
#	Option	    "ForceMonitors" "crt1,crt2"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "75"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

```

### Post by jan. on 2007-07-15
@princemanjee:
You are using the ati driver for GL desktop and fglrx for BigDesktop. Have you tried configuring Xinerama with the ati driver?

---

### Post by princemanjee on 2007-07-15
> **jan. said:**
> @princemanjee:
You are using the ati driver for GL desktop and fglrx for BigDesktop. Have you tried configuring Xinerama with the ati driver?

Thank you for the quick reply and the easy answer is Yes I did try it...  but that is not to say i didn't screw up with my syntax or some other thing... 

If im not mistaken I got some error regarding the use of the "Composite" setting... or an Overlay error...

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-07-15
> **princemanjee said:**
> Thank you for the quick reply and the easy answer is Yes I did try it...  but that is not to say i didn't screw up with my syntax or some other thing... 

If im not mistaken I got some error regarding the use of the "Composite" setting... or an Overlay error...

You must turn composite off for the fglrx driver to work. In a xorg.conf file, any boolean options that's there without any value after it, such as what's highlighted below means that it is enabled.  So you might want to either remove tha tline or put a "0" or "false" after it.
Section "Extensions"
**	Option		"Composite"**
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection

---

### Post by princemanjee on 2007-07-16
Ok i did that and now if I try to enable something simple like desktop effects i get "The Composite extension is not available" in a dialog box...  I know I have tried that before... I attached a copy of the ATI report and I installed the newest ATI drivers (circa 6/25/2007); 

hope that helps you help me :)

thanks for any or all help...

also is it true there is a cap on the max resolution and thats why it wont work?

Thanks!

---

### Post by jan. on 2007-07-17
As far as I know, the default ubuntu desktop effects are based upon AIGLX and the proprietary ATI drivers do not support that as well as for example the NVIDIA drivers do.

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-07-17
> **princemanjee said:**
> Ok i did that and now if I try to enable something simple like desktop effects i get "The Composite extension is not available" in a dialog box...  I know I have tried that before... I attached a copy of the ATI report and I installed the newest ATI drivers (circa 6/25/2007); 

hope that helps you help me :)

thanks for any or all help...

also is it true there is a cap on the max resolution and thats why it wont work?

Thanks!
You need to setup XGL in order to run Desktop effects.  And yes, as far as I know there is a cap on the resolution for dual monitors.  If you don't exceed the limit, then it should work (i've done it before).I think max resolution is 1024x768 *2.

---

### Post by jan. on 2007-07-17
The cap for 1024x768 on each single monitor could indeed be true, since I cannot get XGL running on my Desktop, 1280x1024 each monitor.

---

### Post by ChangHuang on 2007-07-22
Is it possible to get one portrait, one landscape using the ATI binary driver ?

Thanks

ATI binary driver Big-Desktop works on  IBM(Lenovo) Thinkpad T60 with 2 external Dell FP2001 LCD monitors connect through the port replicator one analog and one digital output. 



Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Generic Video Card"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
        Option      "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
        Option      "PairModes" "1280x1024+1280x1024,1400x1050+1400x1050,1600x1200+1600x1200"
        Option      "EnableMonitor" "tmds1,crt1"
        Option      "RandRRotation" "on"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-07-23
> **ChangHuang said:**
> Is it possible to get one portrait, one landscape using the ATI binary driver ?

Thanks

ATI binary driver Big-Desktop works on  IBM(Lenovo) Thinkpad T60 with 2 external Dell FP2001 LCD monitors connect through the port replicator one analog and one digital output. 



Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Generic Video Card"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
        Option      "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
        Option      "PairModes" "1280x1024+1280x1024,1400x1050+1400x1050,1600x1200+1600x1200"
        Option      "EnableMonitor" "tmds1,crt1"
        Option      "RandRRotation" "on"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

I believe that it is possible.  Just change the "PairModes" options to fit the resolutions of your choice.

### Post by paulsonm on 2007-07-26
Need some help here... Just installed Ubuntu for the first time, running 2 1280x1024 monitors on an ATI Radeon X600. Here's my x.org conf file. Right now it's just mirroring the displays at 1280x1024...thanks!

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 51.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies Inc RV380 [Radeon X600 (PCIE)]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO"
	Option	    "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1280x1024+1280x1024"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies Inc RV380 [Radeon X600 (PCIE)]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection

### Post by princemanjee on 2007-07-28
instead of 'PairModes' you want to use something like BigDesktop or Xinerama or Merged...  and you may need to use 2560x1024... if you scroll through my xorg.conf file here you will see my settings I also have a ATI x600 on a Dell GX 620.... also try downloading the ATI Catalyst software its a good jumping point to start from... It includes a GUI to make a lot of the xorg changes for you and it backs up your xorg every time before making changes so if something goes wrong you can replace it with little to know trouble....

Hope this helps...   ( the attachment xorg is with the BigDesktop Setting before i installed the ATI software  in case you dont want to do that and the inline text xorg.conf is configured from the ATI Catalyst GUI)

On a side note... Dell just gave ATI a push to come up with better Ubuntu drivers and is pushing even harder to make them open source so the community can help make them more stable.

You can get the [[COLOR="RoyalBlue"][CENTER][LEFT]_ATI software_[/LEFT][/CENTER][/COLOR]]("https://a248.e.akamai.net/f/674/9206/0/www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/ati-driver-installer-8.39.4-x86.x86_64.run") at: ([https://a248.e.akamai.net/f/674/9206/0/www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/ati-driver-installer-8.39.4-x86.x86_64.run](https://a248.e.akamai.net/f/674/9206/0/www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/ati-driver-installer-8.39.4-x86.x86_64.run))

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 57.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies Inc RV380 [Radeon X600 (PCIE)]"
	Driver      "ati"
	Option	    "MonitorLayout" "LCD, CRT"
	Option	    "CRT2Position" "RightOf"
	Option	    "MetaModes" "1024x768-1024x768"
#	Option	    "MergedXinerama" "on"
	Option	    "MergedNonRectangular" "true"
	Option	    "MergedFB" "true"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
Option "RenderAccel" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
Option "RenderAccel" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
Option "RenderAccel" "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies Inc RV380 [Radeon X600 (PCIE)]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite"
EndSection

```

### Post by servo153 on 2007-08-14
You have provided the answer I have been looking for for a very long time.  I spent a lot of time getting dual monitors to work in Ubuntu but I always had a slight problem no one seemed to have.  Once in awhile the mouse became inaccurate and offset until I dragged it back to the other screen and back again.  Also, the mouse pointer seemed to jump when dragging over windows.  I went through my xorg so many times to try and fix this subtle problem with no luck.  By following these instructions and enabling big desktop, my mouse pointer is accurate and behaves exactly as it should on both screens.  Thank you so much for posting this.  Xinerama by itself was not good enough.

### Post by Ailbe on 2007-08-17
I have an interesting problem I'm hoping someone can help with...  I've tried everything I know, researched all over the web for this.

### Post by metroknight on 2007-08-17
I had the same problem until I realized that until I change my resolution to equal my monitors combined, it was just going to mirror itself. I was running at 1280x1024 and when I changed it to 2560x1024, I got the Ati Big-desktop running. In all the posts that I read and the various guides, I don't remember reading about changing my resolution to the combined number to running it.

> I have an interesting problem I'm hoping someone can help with...  I've tried everything I know, researched all over the web for this.  

I can get my dual screen to work, ONLY at the login screen.  Once I login as my user ID it goes back to the two screens being a mirror of each other...  Really driving me nuts and so far I can't find anyone who has a suggestion of what to do.  

I know a config file is sitting somewhere with all the info to run just like I like it, but when I login something changes.  Any idea what?


If changing the res doesn't work you can manually edit **/home/username/.kde/share/config/displayconfigrc**, change the height and width to your desired size.

### Post by Arituay on 2007-09-09
For anyone still having problems refer to KEinstein's post on page 3 of the thread (post #29).  I had some problems getting this to work then followed that post and now it works.  After I had the fglrx drive installed correctly I copied the relevant sections from his xorg.conf file in that post (though I changed the resolutions to suit my preferences), rebooted, and changed the resolution in System>Preferences>Screen Resolution screen and that got my big desktop working great.

I don't like the aticonfig tool, that never did work correctly for me.  Every time I tried to add a pairmode using aticonfig it wouldn't work, it would always change the pairmode in the xorg.conf file to "" (replacing the pairmode I had manually typed in).  For that reason I recommend not using the aticonfig tool to add pairmodes.

Below is my xorg.conf file with the relevant sections that I copied from KEinstein's post in bold (my xorg.conf file had specific monitor and device names, those wouldn't work, I had to replace those with the items in bold):


```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
# 
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
**	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0**
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

[B]Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection[/B]

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

[B]Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection[/B]

[B]Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1024x768+1024x768"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection[/B]

[B]Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection[/B]

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

[B]Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection[/B]

```

I hope that helps someone.

---

### Post by airdelroy on 2007-09-20
Ok, I am having problems getting this setup just right.  I am real close though.

My main monitor (laptop) is 1680x1050 and my second monitor is 1600x1200.  I have gotten the second to sync at 1600x1200 by opening a range in the refresh/sync lines of my config.  The second monitor is running at 1600x1200, 74.9kHz, 60Hz.  If I enter in these timings in my config it forces the monitor down to 1280x1024.

The problem seems to be that X thinks the second monitor is the same size as the first monitor.  And it is scaling the image on the second monitor.  It thinks the screen is 1680x1050 instead of 1600x1200.  So everything is stretched and looks bad.

Here is what pairmodes I have:

$ aticonfig --list-pairmode
Get pair modes : 1
 0    1680x1050 + 1680x1050 --> 3360x1050

And I cant seem to add this:

$ sudo aticonfig --add-pairmode=1680x1050+1600x1200
FGLRX_AddPairMode failed when try to add mode : 1680x1050+1600x1200
Using xorg.conf
Saved back-up to xorg.conf.fglrx-1

Here is the device section of my xorg.conf:

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option      "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
	#Option      "PairMode"      "1680x1050+1600x1200"
	Option      "Mode2"         "1600x1200" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option      "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,TMDS1"
	#Option      "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,LCD1"
	Option      "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
	Option      "HSync2" "30 - 120" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option      "VRefresh2" "60 - 120" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Ive also tried the commented lines above...


Any ideas on how to fix this?

### Post by googelybear on 2007-10-03
Hi folks, 
I did everything as you told but my 2nd display always shows the same content as my primary screen  ("clone" mode instead of "extend desktop").
Here's a snippet from my xorg.conf (full version attached):
```
Section "Device"
        Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. M10 NT [FireGL Mobility T2]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
        Option      "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
        Option      "PairModes" "1600x1200+1600x1200,0x0+0x0"
        Option      "EnableMonitor" "lvds,crt1"
        Option      "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
        Option      "ForceMonitors" "lvds,crt1"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

``` I am using a Thinkpad T42p (nvidia FireGL Mobility T2 graphics card) and Ubuntu 7.04. Does anyone know what's wrong here? thx alot.

edit: When I use the snippet from the first page of this topic the login-screen extends (!) to the second monitor, but after I typ in username/pw it goes back to clone mode ;(

### Post by bir7 on 2007-10-05
googelybear: The Option "PairModes" should be "PairMode"  without the "s"

### Post by knichel on 2007-10-18
Thanks for the How-To.  I have been fighting with this for weeks.  I finally have it working, but one thing is wrong.  I configure everything but when I restart the computer my settings are wacked... I have to reload a previous xorg.conf and run the aticonfig dual head statement...  

Here is my xorg.conf that is currently working...
```


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	Screen         "aticonfig-Screen[1]" RightOf "Default Screen"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	VertRefresh  60.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies Inc RV350 [Mobility Radeon 9600 M10]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
	Option	    "PairModes" "1280x800+1280x800,1280x800+1024x768"
	Option	    "EnableMonitor" "crt1,lvds"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies Inc RV350 [Mobility Radeon 9600 M10]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection

```

Once I restart X with this config, I still need to enter this command...
```
sudo aticonfig --initial=dual-head --screen-layout=left --vrefresh=1,60
```

How can I get this set up so the settings stick when I restart my computer?

Thanks in  advance...
Mike

### Post by ccrs8 on 2007-10-20
I just upgraded to 7.10 this morning, and found that the dual-monitor setup that I had gotten working via the instructions in this thread (Big-Desktop using the aticonfig tool) did not carry over.  I didn't think it would be a problem, as I could just repeat these instructions.  However, unfortunately there seems to be something wrong with 7.10 and the aticonfig tool, as every time I ran it, there were error messages involving invalid pointers.  The errors happened after saving the old xorg.conf file to a backup but before creating a valid new xorg.conf file, so after running the aticonfig tool, there was no working xorg.conf file.  Not too good if you hope to have a working X session.

Anyway, I got things working by following the manual configuration instructions.  I honestly have no idea what I copied and pasted in to my conf file, but it worked and I'm happy.  It seems that 7.10's "System->Administration->Screens and Graphics" doesn't work with this method, as it only shows one screen and I was never able to get it to recognize a second screen, so at least for 7.10, it's still manual xorg.conf editing for ATI Big-Desktop users.

---

I didn't think it would be a problem, as I could just repeat these instructions.  However, unfortunately there seems to be something wrong with 7.10 and the aticonfig tool, as every time I ran it, there were error messages involving invalid pointers.  The errors happened after saving the old xorg.conf file to a backup but before creating a valid new xorg.conf file, so after running the aticonfig tool, there was no working xorg.conf file.  Not too good if you hope to have a working X session.

Anyway, I got things working by following the manual configuration instructions.  I honestly have no idea what I copied and pasted in to my conf file, but it worked and I'm happy.  It seems that 7.10's "System->Administration->Screens and Graphics" doesn't work with this method, as it only shows one screen and I was never able to get it to recognize a second screen, so at least for 7.10, it's still manual xorg.conf editing for ATI Big-Desktop users.

Eh...anyone have any idea how the conf file should be edited to get this to work in 7.10? Really wouldn't want to downgrade to 7.04

---

### Post by ccrs8 on 2007-10-20
> **vdox said:**
> Eh...anyone have any idea how the conf file should be edited to get this to work in 7.10? Really wouldn't want to downgrade to 7.04

In the initial post on this thread, I simply added to my xorg.conf file what I was told to add to the "Device" section.  It is in the part of the post under the message that states "The following is how to manually configure Xorg.conf file. It will soon become obsolete (I just need more input from those who have test the previous method)."

To start from the beginning, this is how I got my dual monitor to work from the moment I realized that it was broken with the upgrade from 7.04 to 7.10:

1) Follow the instructions on this post's mother post, located at: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=221174](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=221174)

2) Intended on following the instructions in this thread using the aticonfig tool, but it didn't work (my experience using this is in a post a few posts above this.

3) Used the manual xorg.conf editing method described in the section that claims is or will be shortly obsolete.  Restarted X, worked like a charm.  You may have to go in to the screen resolution tool to switch to the proper resolution (when I did this once, I had to change the resolution manually to 2560x1024, but when I did this again, I didn't have to).

LIke I said before, I am no expert.  I'm a trial and error (mostly error) type of guy who backed up his xorg.conf file and then blindly copied-and-pasted some text that looked like gibberish in to my xorg.conf file and got lucky.  If the aticonfig tool gets working with 7.10, I'd much prefer to do it that way.  Even better, if the new Screen and Graphics tool would work with ATI proprietary driver and Big-Desktop, that would be the best.

### Post by hypersoar on 2007-10-22
I tried setting this up following the steps in the mother FAQ (running the commands outside of X11). When I ran step 3 (the first step after enabling the fglrx driver), the output said that it would affect the current session.

The Screens and Graphics seems to cause most people only problems, and I'm one of them. They shouldn't have released such a poorly-tested tool.

I've been trying to enable a dual-monitor setup. I have two CRTs hooked up to a Radeon 9550.

When I run the monitor query, it didn't work at all (don't remember the output). If I run it inside X11, I get this:
```
  Connected monitors: none
  Enabled monitors: none

```
I can circumvent the problem inside X11 by adding DISPLAY=:0 to the beginning of the command. When I try using that output to enable the setup, I get this output (don't remember it exactly):
```
Unable to enable monitor '`.
```
If I run startx, I get a blank screen and I'm forced to reboot.

### Post by Leprechaun on 2007-10-22
Thanks to this guide, I managed to get BigDesktop working, with one remaining glitch, in 7.10. I'm hoping someone can help me out with my bump in the road. Relevant system information:

Graphics Card: ATI Technologies Inc RV530 [Radeon X1600]
Monitor 1: Westinghouse LCM-19w4 (a widescreen LCD)
Monitor 2: ViewSonic A90f+ (a CRT)

The problem I'm having is that BigDesktop will not let the CRT monitor have its own screen resolution; DesktopSetup "horizontal" just doubles the size of the widescreen monitor. Here's my working xorg.conf:

```

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier     "Default Layout"
        Screen         "LCD Screen" 0 0
        InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
        FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load  "i2c"
        Load  "bitmap"
        Load  "ddc"
        Load  "dri"
        Load  "extmod"
        Load  "freetype"
        Load  "glx"
        Load  "int10"
        Load  "type1"
        Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option      "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"
        Option      "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "LCD Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "CRT Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "LCD Device"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
        Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,CRT"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "LCD Screen"
        Device     "LCD Device"
        Monitor    "LCD Monitor"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
                Modes    "1440x900"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

This creates a virtual screen with a resolution of 2880x900 with a refresh rate of 60. It stretches across both of my monitors and looks quite nifty. Now I'd like to change my CRT screen so the picture will display without being stretched (and so I can up the refresh rate to 70). I modify the Device section to read as follows:

```
Section "Device"
        Identifier  "LCD Device"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
        Option      "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,CRT"
        **Option      "PairModes" "1440x900+1152x864"**
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection
```

This causes X to crash. I receive the following backtrace just afte (or during, hard to tell which) the calculation of the screen resolution modelines:

```
Backtrace:
0: /usr/bin/X(xf86SigHandler+0x81) [0x80c9581]
1: [0xffffe420]
2: /usr/lib/xorg/modules/drivers//fglrx_drv.so [0xb79e2b53]
3: /usr/lib/xorg/modules/drivers//fglrx_drv.so [0xb79de1a1]
4: /usr/lib/xorg/modules/drivers//fglrx_drv.so(atiddxPreInit+0x988) [0xb79d8a48]
5: /usr/bin/X(InitOutput+0x9a4) [0x80a8e54]
6: /usr/bin/X(main+0x27b) [0x8076ceb]
7: /lib/tls/i686/cmov/libc.so.6(__libc_start_main+0xe0) [0xb7dcc050]
8: /usr/bin/X(FontFileCompleteXLFD+0x1e1) [0x8076241]

Fatal server error:
Caught signal 11.  Server aborting
```

X then proceeds to start in low-resolution mode (thank you, 7.10).

Has anyone encountered any errors when using the PairModes option? I've been fiddling with this config for a few days now, and this is the closest I've managed to get.

If anyone would like more info, just let me know.

Thanks,
Leprechaun

```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

### Post by Ozsmoka on 2007-10-22
Thankyou ccrs8, 

You definitely put me on the right path, And a huge thanks to all that have contributed to this post including, of course, the OP. I now have Big Desk top working ala Feisty.

One thing I will note for some one else who may have the same problem that I had. 

When adding the above mentioned code [soon to be obsolete lol], all went fine and I got Big Desk top straight away, but the screen resolution defaulted to 3548x1344 for some reason, when what I wanted was 2560x1024.

Im no expert, like ccrs8, Im more error than trial, but I first tried to add 

** modeline  "2560x1024@60" 266.95 2560 2200 2424 2800 1024 1537 1540 1589 -hsync +vsync**

to the monitor section and this had no effect.

Then I noticed that, directly after that section,

[B]Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"L1919S"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Virtual	3548   1344[/B]. 

when I changed this to 

             **virtual 1280   1024**

everything was fine.

Thanks to all

cheers

---

### Post by sloggerkhan on 2007-10-22
I had perfect wide desktop under feisty, but now with gutsy things aren't so hot. If I have the external display plugged in at boot, it defaults to a mirror of the laptop panel only it has bright pixels and artifacts. Needless to say, I unplugged it because I was afraid it was frying or something. I tried using the my old xorg.conf from from feisty, but it didn't work. Anyhow, I think I'm through messing for now. I'll wait for the fglrx 8.42.x to try messing again.

I have to say that I was shocked that the default settings could be so bad that they started screwing up an external display using mirror mode.

### Post by ChangHuang on 2007-10-23
upgraded from 7.04  to 7.10

Computer: Lenovo T60 Laptop using port replicator with  analog and  DVI outputs.
Monitors: 2 Dell FP2001 one  using analog port and the other  digital
xorg-server 2:1.3.0.0.dfsg-12ubuntu8
 kernel :2.6.22-14-generic #1 SMP

xorg.conf crashed after the upgrade. In my case is the 
      Option      "PairModes" "1280x1024+1280x1024,1400x1050+1400x1050,1600x1200+1600x1200"
that caused the crash.

I can use the the same xorg.conf file from  7.04 into 7.10 by leaving out the PairModes  line in "Device"

I also added some more modules from the xorg.conf.failsave  under "Modules"

        Load  "int10"
        Load  "dbe"
        Load  "fbdevhw"
        Load  "record"

in my Xorg.0.log fglrx probes the following resolution 
(--) fglrx(0): Virtual size is 1600x1200 (pitch 0)
.
.
(--) fglrx(0): Virtual size is 3200x1200 (pitch 3200)




Also it is necessary to change the resolution either under
System-->Preferences-->Screen Resolution or
System--> Administration-->Screens and Graphics

The Screen and Graphics preference tool shows only Screen 1 is the
Default screen with resolution 3200x1200
screen2 is Disabled







Here is my xorg.conf

        Load  "int10"
        Load  "dbe"
        Load  "fbdevhw"
        Load  "record"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver      "synaptics"
        Option      "SendCoreEvents" "true"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/psaux"
        Option      "Protocol" "auto-dev"
        Option      "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Generic Video Card"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
        Option      "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
#       Option      "PairModes" "1280x1024+1280x1024,1400x1050+1400x1050,1600x1200+1600x1200"
        Option      "EnableMonitor" "tmds1,crt1"
        Option      "RandRRotation" "on"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Default Screen"
        Device     "Generic Video Card"
        Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     1
                Modes    "1400x1050" "(pitch" "1400)"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     4
                Modes "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     8
                Modes "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     15
                Modes "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     16
                Modes "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     24
                Modes "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
                Modes    "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "0"
EndSection

Yes, It works.
By switching to xgl

apt-get install  xserver-xgl
and use the compiz config manager
aptget install compizconfig-settings-manager

### Post by bbbaldie on 2007-10-25
:popcorn:[I]One thing I will note for some one else who may have the same problem that I had.

When adding the above mentioned code [soon to be obsolete lol], all went fine and I got Big Desk top straight away, but the screen resolution defaulted to 3548x1344 for some reason, when what I wanted was 2560x1024.

Im no expert, like ccrs8, Im more error than trial, but I first tried to add

modeline "2560x1024@60" 266.95 2560 2200 2424 2800 1024 1537 1540 1589 -hsync +vsync

to the monitor section and this had no effect.

Then I noticed that, directly after that section,

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Device "Generic Video Card"
Monitor "L1919S"
Defaultdepth 24
SubSection "Display"
Depth 24
Virtual 3548 1344.

when I changed this to

virtual 1280 1024

everything was fine.[/I]

Your tweak to xorg.conf ended eight hours of INTENSE work to get 2056 x 1024 resolution on a Gutsy install on a Dell GX620 and a radeon x600 card.

THANK YOU!!!

### Post by ccrs8 on 2007-10-25
> **bbbaldie said:**
> 

Your tweak to xorg.conf ended eight hours of INTENSE work to get 2056 x 1024 resolution on a Gutsy install on a Dell GX620 and a radeon x600 card.

THANK YOU!!!

bbbaldie,

It looks like you and I have the exact same setup - GX620, radeon x600 card.  I'd be interested in seeing your xorg.conf file, as it looks like you've set things up slightly different than I have.

Are you able to get desktop effects to work?  I'm not terribly interested in actually having them activated at all times, but I wouldn't mind checking them out and at least seeing what all the hype is about.  However, I am unable to get them to work.  I'm not sure if this is a limitation of Big-Desktop, our graphics card, the proprietary driver, or my xorg.conf setup.

I would say that, at least with my specific combo of monitors, driver, card, and setup, display-related issues are the biggest qualms that I have about the upgrade from Feisty to Gutsy.  Everything else seems the same or improved, but between display instabilities (lots of screen warping that I can only get to go away by clicking "Test" in the Screens and Graphics control panel), the difficulty in setting up the dual monitors (which were already set up fine with Feisty but got blown away during the upgrade), and the seemingly complete lack of compatibility with the new "Screens and Graphics" control panel (the Test button is the only button that seems to be useful, and that's just to refresh the screen and get rid of the warping), I've been fairly disappointed in this area.  Not that I care much - I've got no problem fiddling, and it still beat the pants off of any other offerings.

### Post by twizler on 2007-10-25
THANK GOODNESS !!!! IT WORKS 

ati dual-head monitor -- projectors and any other damn thing with ati that needs a second display. 

ANSWER  FOUND AT: [http://www.lunders-and.no/wp/?p=96](http://www.lunders-and.no/wp/?p=96)

[SIZE="5"][COLOR="Red"]aticonfig --query-monitor
Connected monitors: none
Enabled monitors: none[/COLOR][/SIZE]
[COLOR="Red"][SIZE="3"][SIZE="7"]FIXED MY NIGHTMARE[/SIZE] [/SIZE]
[anders@lon ~]$ [COLOR="Red"][SIZE="5"]export DISPLAY=:0[/SIZE][/COLOR]
[anders@lon ~]$ aticonfig --query-monitor[/COLOR]
_____
After several days of struggling with enabling my external display (video projector), using the binary fglrx driver in Ubuntu Gutsy, I’ll just write down the trick I’ve used to fix this.

I was puzzled by the fact that aticonfig didn’t show me any info about connected monitors on my Lenovo Thinkpad T60. All I got was

[anders@lon ~]$ aticonfig --query-monitor
Connected monitors: none
Enabled monitors: none

What I should have realized early on is the fact that I’m using Xgl, in addition to X. This gives me extra visual effects with the Compiz system, but X is running on DISPLAY :0 and Xgl is running on :1. So when I use aticonfig on DISPLAY :1 it doesn’t give me the correct information. To solve this I do

[anders@lon ~]$ export DISPLAY=:0
[anders@lon ~]$ aticonfig --query-monitor
Connected monitors: crt1, lvds
Enabled monitors: lvds

Now aticonfig gives me the correct information, and I’m also able to do aticonfig --enable-monitor=crt1,lvds to enable my external monitor. Finally!

---

### Post by twizler on 2007-10-27
THANK GOD IT WORKED!!! GOOD HOW TO. 
WHAT I HAD TO DO. 

My dual-head ati card annoyance  
Screen window would maximize on both monitors HUGE. 
> If you are running compiz already with ati big desktop ...and when you do aticonfig you get [COLOR="Red"]
Connected monitor none 
 Enabled monitors: none
[/COLOR]e ... in terminal. 
[COLOR="Red"]export Display=:0[/COLOR]
[B]
The fix was  SO SIMPLE [/B]
> sudo aticonfig --query-monitor
OUTPUT -- 
[COLOR="Blue"]  Connected monitors: [COLOR="Red"]crt1[/COLOR],[COLOR="Red"] crt2[/COLOR]
  **Enabled monitors: crt1, crt2[/COLOR]**

This tells me that ati thinks i have to montiors ...  crt1 and crt2 

THE COMMAND THAT MADE IT WORK. 

>  **sudo aticonfig --enable-monitor=crt1,crt2**

This made my one monitor go black and bounced me out of big desktop in cloned mode.
I then did the restart xorg.conf file command that got me back to the login screen. 
> CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE
 
> Log out login ubuntu
clicked -->preferences-->resolutions -->display - "LARGE big desktop mode

NOW IT WORKS LIKE I WANT IT TO NO MORE maximize on both screens. ( works like a dream) 
Now when i use big desktop and maximize a window it goes to the correct size of the monitor i am in .. maximize to size of monitor 1 move screen over to monitor 2 hit maximize and it maximizes to mon2. NOT both.

---

### Post by cmcginty on 2007-10-30
The default steps did not work for my card (ATI X300SE). I was able to get much better results from the [Gentoo instructions]("http://gentoo-wiki.com/HOWTO_Dual_Monitors#With_the_proprietary_binary_driver").

The main steps I used were:

```
# aticonfig --initial=dual-head --dtop=horizontal --screen-layout=right -v
```

So here is my simplified xorg.conf as well:
```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Layout0"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	RgbPath      "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "dbe"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "dri"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Mouse0"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Keyboard0"
	Driver      "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

```

Good Luck !!!

### Post by heebus on 2007-10-30
Twizzler's fix worked for me.  I just had to add the startup command:

  export DISPLAY=:0

I could of even added it to my profile, which makes more sense.
