### Post by frogotronic on 2008-05-30
> **gfxguy said:**
> Ok, I've run through both the MergedFB and BigDesktop instructions, and can't quite get what I want.

It's on a laptop, with Radeon 200M card; the laptop display is 1280x800, the external monitor is 1280x1024.

Using the described methods, I can only get 1024x768 showing up on the external monitor.

The xorg.conf below is completely roll-your-own, and it's the best I've been able to get so far.  I'd appreciate any suggestions on how to get a big desktop, full resolution on the external monitor.

The configuration below is the only way I've been able to get full resolution on the external monitor, but it creates two desktops, and I can't drag and drop between the two, or move application from one to another; it's almost like being logged into two machines.  Worst, if I'm working on one display for too long, the screen saver kicks in on the other and just screws everything up... I can't even load the screensaver control panel.

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Card0"
#	VendorName	"ATI"
#	BoardName	"Radeon X200 (Laptop)"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen		0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Card1"
#	VendorName	"ATI"
#	BoardName	"Radeon X200 (External)"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen		1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"LaptopMonitor"
	VendorName	"Toshiba"
	ModelName	"Built In 1280x800"
EndSection

Section  "Monitor"
	Identifier	"ExternalMonitor"
	VendorName	"Samsung"
	ModelName	"713v"
	HorizSync	30.0 - 81.0
	VertRefresh	56.0 - 75.0
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen0"
	Device		"Card0"
	Monitor		"LaptopMonitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes	"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen1"
	Device		"Card1"
	Monitor		"ExternalMonitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Screen0"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Multihead Layout"
	Screen		0 "Screen0"
	Screen		1 "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"DefaultServerLayout" "Multihead Layout"
EndSection

```

### Post by gfxguy on 2008-06-04
For the last couple of posters; I'm using an ATI card with fglrx, but it might apply to your situations... the advice that cement_head gave me basically showed to not try to force the resolution.

I don't know why.

My config (don't copy this directly to your config file, it won't work without some editing), doesn't specify the full desktop size... somehow X just knows; the auto detection of the monitor seems to work.
```

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "Capabilities" "0x00000800"
	Option	    "MonitorLayout" "AUTO,LVDS"
	Option      "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
	Option	    "HSync2" "31-80"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "56-76"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ"
	BusID       "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```

The screen actually refers to only the laptop's built in screen; I'd assume that'd be your primary monitor on a desktop.  Notice there's no pair-modes listed.  While there's no second monitor section, there IS HSync2 and Vrefresh2 options set in the device...

I tweaked it for hours and hours trying to force a mode to work, and based on cement_head's config, discovered I was putting too much in; it actually came down to taking stuff out and letting things just work on their own.

One of my major issues was simply that add-pairmode would fail, as would setting resolution=1,WxH, it would say there's no second monitor... adding a second monitor section didn't seem to help.

Intuitively, I'd think you create two monitors, then tell X how to arrange them.  When I tried that, I got display :0 and :1, which is not what I wanted at all.

One of the other things his conf had that mine didn't was that I was using LVDS (the laptop built in screen) with crt1... based on his config, I used "AUTO" instead (on the monitor layout option).

Good luck; this was really frustrating for me... I love working in Linux, but hate when things don't "just work."  I'm a website and graphics developer... not a system administrator, and I don't want to be one any more than I have to be.

Oh, and in case it would help, here is my current xorg.conf after the driver-reinstall and with the settings back to how I describe them above (one monitor gets the right resolution, the other doesn't):

```

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"dbe"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"Buttons"	"7"
	Option		"ButtonMapping"	"1 2 3 6 7"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"DELL 2208WFP"
	Vendorname	"Generic LCD Display"
	Modelname	"LCD Panel 1680x1050"
	Horizsync	31.5-65.5
	Vertrefresh	56.0	-	65.0
  modeline  "1680x1050@60" 147.14 1680 1784 1968 2256 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc ATI Default Card"
	Boardname	"ati"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Screen	0
	Option		"DesktopSetup"	"horizontal"#Enable Big Desktop
	Option		"Mode2"	"1680x1050"#Resolution for second monitor
	Option		"DesktopSetup"	"LVDS,AUTO"#the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option		"HSync2"	"65"#This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option		"VRefresh2"	"60"#This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
	Option		"PairMode"	"1680x1050+1680x1050"
	Option		"VideoOverlay"	"on"
	Option		"OpenGLOverlay"	"off"
	
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc ATI Default Card"
	Monitor		"DELL 2208WFP"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1680x1050@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"Disable"
EndSection

```

This is my first entry in the forum. Anyways, I want to share my big desktop settings with you since it is working fine now since a couple of days. I think I was reading this entire forum to get it work.

My system:

Hardy Heron
Acer - Extensa 4100
ATI X700 Mobility

Internal screen: 1280x800
External Screen: 1280x1024



[INDENT][INDENT]Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "de"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver "fglrx"
	Option "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option "OpenGLOverlay" "off"

        # --- The following was added for dual-head support:
        Option "DesktopSetup" "horizontal" # Enable Big Desktop
        Option "PairMode" "1280x800+1280x1024" # Pair mode resolutions
        Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO"
        #Option "HSync2" "65.29" #The horizontal sync for the secondary display.
        #Option "VRefresh2" "59.954" #The refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
        Device "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Monitor "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth 24
        SubSection "Display"
        	Viewport 0 0
        	Depth 24
        	Virtual 1280 1024 # Note the resolutions in this line
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions" 
	Option "Composite" "0" 
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"

        Option        "AIGLX"            "off"

EndSection

[/INDENT][/INDENT]

As you can see the file is pretty short now... but still... it works fine for me. 

Hope somebody can make us of it.

**And here my question to the community:** Has anyone succeeded to use Compiz in big desktop mode for a total resolution of 2560x1024?

Yours

Jagal

### Post by pmaciver on 2008-07-09
Well I did a lot of reading on the topic yesterday, and I am glad to say that I have got big desktop working now.

I am not using the drivers from ATI any more I am using the open source ati driver that comes with ubuntu and also xrandr. These 2 links help me out a lot.

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RadeonDriver](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RadeonDriver)
[http://wiki.x.org/wiki/Projects/XRandR](http://wiki.x.org/wiki/Projects/XRandR)

I could have saved myself many hours if I had known about this from the beginning. But I guess even a old hand like me can always learn something about Linux. 

By the way I have a Radeon 2600 HD card. If you have a radeon card also I suggest that you give this a try, took me 10 minutes from start to finish (after reading through all the docs first of course) to get this working.

Now I will try to get compiz working with it (I think I read it is possible). If I do I will post what steps I used to get that going also.

Finally my dreams will stopped being dominated by how I will get this done, lol.

Hope this helps someone.

### Post by Chachee on 2008-07-10
So I got mine to work as well.  However I didn't use xrandr.  Instead I was playing around with the ATI Catalyst Control Center (ATICC) and seeing what settings were in there.  Then I would replace the xorg.conf file with a backup I made of my good settings.

I selected big desktop originally and it threw me to the 1024x768 resolution.  So I replaced it with my backup.

I unenabled my DVI port, and renabled it.  Then I tried big desktop again.  I accepted the changes, but before I restarted X I replaced the xorg.conf with my back up.  Low and behold I have my dual screens.  ATICC says the resolution is 3360x1050.

It's awesome on my 2 24in Dells.  However I miss my Compiz, which I actually had set up and was making navigation faster.  

I get the same thing you do:

```
jeremy@jeremy-desktop:~$ glxinfo | grep direct
direct rendering: No (LIBGL_ALWAYS_INDIRECT set)

```

Here's my current xorg.conf-

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"fglrx"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Defaultdepth	24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection
```

I'm going to restart and see what happens.  This could all be filed under "almost too good to be true," especially since the xorg.conf is very basic.

JT

EDIT - persists across reboots.  Now just have to figure compiz out.

### Post by Chachee on 2008-07-10
Some simple research shows that for compiz direct rendering should be set to 'no' for compiz effects.  

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/desktop-effects/+bug/137388](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/desktop-effects/+bug/137388)


I don't even know where to look for settings on this.  I'll look around more on the internet.

Do you think this should be a separate thread though?

Hmmm.. ok I used this site to set up compiz originally.  The person has a script compiz-check which check's compiz compatibility.  

[http://forlong.blogage.de/article/pages/Compiz-Check](http://forlong.blogage.de/article/pages/Compiz-Check)

Here's my output - 

```
jeremy@jeremy-desktop:~$ ./compiz-check

Gathering information about your system...

 Distribution:          Ubuntu 8.04
 Desktop environment:   GNOME
 Graphics chip:         ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]
 Driver in use:         fglrx
 Rendering method:      AIGLX

Checking if it's possible to run Compiz on your system...

 Checking for texture_from_pixmap...               [ OK ]
 Checking for non power of two support...          [ OK ]
 Checking for composite extension...               [ OK ]
 Checking for FBConfig...                          [ OK ]
 Checking for hardware/setup problems...           [FAIL]

There has been (at least) one error detected with your setup:
 Error: Your current resolution is too high to run Compiz. 

Would you like to know more? (Y/n) y

 Your resolution is 3360x1050 but the maximum 3D texture size that your
 graphics card is capable of is 2048x2048. Thus Compiz will not be able to run
 on this setup. You have to decrease the resolution first (in case you are
 using a dual-head setup, try disabling one monitor and run the script again).
```


HAHAHHAHAHAHAHAH

JT

---

### Post by pmaciver on 2008-07-11
Just another discovery, I have got the bigdesktop working with the amd drivers now. 

I decided to do what other people have done and do a fresh install, then install the amd drivers and no others. The I did a aticonfig --initial, restarted x, used the amdcccle to configure bigdesktop. It then told me to restart my machine. And when I did everything was working fine.

Strange, after all that work, this is all it took to get it working. Must have been something to do with upgrading from 7.10 to 8.04 that screws things up.

Anyway, this might help someone so I thought that I'd post it.

Oh and compiz works fine also.

So I would say don't use any other driver just the one from amd (if you have that sort of card) and you should be fine

---

I then tried to set it up using aticonfig, I managed to get dual head To make a long story short, there are several things that make me believe there is something wrong with the correct recognition of my second screen.

1If I have the fglrx ATI propietary driver in use, xrandr does only recognize one monitor.
2After setting up a dual head configuration with aticonfig, my second screen, which is exactly the same as my first screen, works idependly but at incorrect resolution. Changing the resolution with

aticonfig ---resolution=1,1440x900

results in no change and the reply of aticonfig is that screen1 does not exist

aticonfig ---resolution=0,1440x900

does the job but sinc I have identical monitors and my first monitor was already OK, I do not know how it affected my first monitor.

3 Setting up Bigdesktop always results in clones, I cannot get screen 2 to behave differently than one. 


Since Bigdesktop should allow dragging windows from screen to screen, this is what I actually prefer so if anybody has a clue what I might try...

Thank you!

Since Bigdesktop should allow dragging windows from screen to screen, this is what I actually prefer so if anybody has a clue what I might try...

Thank you!

All I can say is reinstall, I spent many days banging my head against the wall with this one, and the only thing that worked was reinstalling. I guess it is something to do with monitor recognition when it is installing. I always have my home directory on a separate partition so that I can reinstall whenever I like and not lose any settings. This is what I did.

1. reinstalled Ubuntu and kept my home partition 

2. logged in, didn't install any other drivers, just went to the amd website and downloaded and installed their drivers.

3. restarted

4. Ran aticonfig --initial

5. restarted x

6. launched amdcccle (or whatever it is called) program that allows the configuration of the screens.

7. configured big desktop. It told me that the with of the desktop was going to be smaller than my 2 screens (i.e. 1600 x 2) but just ignore that. It then tells you to restart the machine.

8. restarted and I had big desktop at maximum resolution. Not once did I touch my xorg.config (the way it should be).

And that was it, compiz works, everything works. Just wish I had done it at the start I would have saved many hours.
